en.motivation: To study family-level topology in topologically ordered phases and provide explicit lattice realizations and diagnostics.

idea_spark.inspiration: Combining pumping processes with tensor-network representations offers new tools for classifying topological phases.

papers.title: Joint Design of Piggyback and Conjugate Transformation Functions for Repair Bandwidth Reduction in Piggybacking Codes
papers.summary: Efficient node repair is a central requirement in distributed storage systems, particularly in high-rate erasure-coded deployments where repair traffic directly affects network overhead and recovery cost. Piggybacking codes reduce the repair bandwidth of MDS array codes while keeping the sub-packetization level small. However, existing piggybacking constructions often rely on restrictive piggyback-function designs to preserve the MDS property over small fields, which limits their repair-bandwidth reduction. We propose {\em conjugate-piggybacking} codes, a new class of MDS array codes that jointly design piggyback functions and conjugate transformations under small sub-packetization. The proposed construction improves repair efficiency while preserving the MDS property over moderate field sizes. In particular, it enables some parity nodes to achieve optimal repair bandwidth and reduces the overall repair bandwidth compared with existing piggybacking-based designs. We analyze the MDS property and repair bandwidth of the proposed codes and evaluate them against existing piggybacking codes under high-code-rate settings over $\mathbb{F}_{2^8}$. We further conduct a repair-traffic simulation under uniform single-node failures to quantify the expected traffic reduction in storage-oriented settings. The results show that our construction consistently achieves lower repair bandwidth than related piggybacking codes and reduces expected repair traffic compared with conventional RS repair. These gains are obtained at the cost of a slightly larger field size, revealing a practical trade-off between repair efficiency and field-size overhead for high-rate distributed storage.

ai.tldr: 提出共轭捎带码，通过联合设计捎带函数和共轭变换，在小子包化下减少高码率分布式存储系统的修复带宽。
ai.motivation: 现有捎带码为保持小域上的MDS性质而限制捎带函数设计，导致修复带宽降低有限。
ai.method: 联合设计捎带函数与共轭变换，在中等域大小下保持MDS性质并提升修复效率。
ai.result: 部分校验节点达到最优修复带宽，整体修复带宽低于现有捎带码，预期修复流量低于传统RS修复。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何通过共轭捎带码在存储系统中平衡修复效率与域大小开销。

idea_spark.idea: 利用共轭变换打破传统捎带函数限制，在中等域上实现更优修复带宽。
idea_spark.risk: 域大小增加可能带来计算和存储开销，实际部署需权衡。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将代数变换与编码结构联合优化，为高码率存储系统提供新设计范式。

en.tldr: Proposes conjugate-piggybacking codes that jointly design piggyback functions and conjugate transformations to reduce repair bandwidth in high-rate distributed storage under small sub-packetization.
en.motivation: Existing piggybacking codes restrict piggyback-function design to preserve MDS property over small fields, limiting repair-bandwidth reduction.
en.method: Jointly design piggyback functions and conjugate transformations to preserve MDS property over moderate field sizes while improving repair efficiency.
en.result: Some parity nodes achieve optimal repair bandwidth; overall repair bandwidth is lower than existing piggybacking codes, and expected repair traffic is reduced compared to conventional RS repair.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how conjugate-piggybacking codes balance repair efficiency and field-size overhead in storage systems.

idea_spark.idea: Use conjugate transformations to break traditional piggyback-function constraints, achieving better repair bandwidth over moderate fields.
idea_spark.risk: Increased field size may introduce computational and storage overhead, requiring trade-off in deployment.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining algebraic transformations with coding structure offers a new design paradigm for high-rate storage systems.

papers.title: Mantis: Mamba-native Tuning is Efficient for 3D Point Cloud Foundation Models
papers.summary: Pre-trained 3D point cloud foundation models (PFMs) have demonstrated strong transferability across diverse downstream tasks. However, full fine-tuning these models is computationally expensive and storage-intensive. Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) offers a promising alternative, but existing PEFT approaches are primarily designed for Transformer-based backbones and rely on token-level prompting or feature transformation. Mamba-based backbones introduce a granularity mismatch between token-level adaptation and state-level sequence dynamics. Consequently, straightforward transfer of existing PEFT approaches to frozen Mamba backbones leads to substantial accuracy degradation and unstable optimization. To address this issue, we propose Mantis, the first Mamba-native PEFT framework for 3D PFMs. Specifically, a State-Aware Adapter (SAA) is introduced to inject lightweight task-conditioned control signals into selective state-space updates, enabling state-level adaptation while keeping the pre-trained backbone frozen. Moreover, different valid point cloud serializations are regularized by Dual-Serialization Consistency Distillation (DSCD), thereby reducing serialization-induced instability. Extensive experiments across multiple benchmarks demonstrate that our Mantis achieves competitive performance with only about 5% trainable parameters. Our code is available at https://github.com/gzhhhhhhh/Mantis.

ai.tldr: 提出首个面向Mamba架构的3D点云基础模型参数高效微调框架Mantis，仅需约5%可训练参数即可达到竞争性能。
ai.motivation: 现有参数高效微调方法主要针对Transformer骨干设计，直接迁移至Mamba骨干会导致精度下降和优化不稳定，因为存在token级适配与状态级序列动态之间的粒度不匹配。
ai.method: 设计状态感知适配器（SAA）向选择性状态空间更新注入轻量任务条件控制信号，实现冻结骨干下的状态级适配；同时引入双序列一致性蒸馏（DSCD）正则化不同点云序列化结果，减少序列化引起的不稳定性。
ai.result: 在多个基准上，Mantis仅用约5%可训练参数即取得与全微调竞争的性能。
ai.help_to_user: 若您需要高效微调3D点云Mamba模型，Mantis提供了即插即用的状态级适配方案，显著降低存储和计算开销。

idea_spark.idea: 将双序列一致性蒸馏扩展为多视角或多模态一致性正则化，进一步提升Mamba骨干在非序列化数据上的泛化能力。
idea_spark.risk: 状态级适配可能对长序列或高维状态空间引入额外计算开销，需权衡效率与性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴Mamba的状态空间动态特性，设计更细粒度的适配机制，启发其他序列模型（如RWKV）的微调方法。

zh.tldr: 提出首个面向Mamba架构的3D点云基础模型参数高效微调框架Mantis，仅需约5%可训练参数即可达到竞争性能。
zh.motivation: 现有参数高效微调方法主要针对Transformer骨干设计，直接迁移至Mamba骨干会导致精度下降和优化不稳定，因为存在token级适配与状态级序列动态之间的粒度不匹配。
zh.method: 设计状态感知适配器（SAA）向选择性状态空间更新注入轻量任务条件控制信号，实现冻结骨干下的状态级适配；同时引入双序列一致性蒸馏（DSCD）正则化不同点云序列化结果，减少序列化引起的不稳定性。
zh.result: 在多个基准上，Mantis仅用约5%可训练参数即取得与全微调竞争的性能。
zh.help_to_user: 若您需要高效微调3D点云Mamba模型，Mantis提供了即插即用的状态级适配方案，显著降低存储和计算开销。

idea_spark.idea: 将双序列一致性蒸馏扩展为多视角或多模态一致性正则化，进一步提升Mamba骨干在非序列化数据上的泛化能力。
idea_spark.risk: 状态级适配可能对长序列或高维状态空间引入额外计算开销，需权衡效率与性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴Mamba的状态空间动态特性，设计更细粒度的适配机制，启发其他序列模型（如RWKV）的微调方法。

en.tldr: Proposes Mantis, the first Mamba-native parameter-efficient fine-tuning framework for 3D point cloud foundation models, achieving competitive performance with only about 5% trainable parameters.
en.motivation: Existing PEFT methods are designed for Transformer backbones and suffer from granularity mismatch when transferred to Mamba backbones, leading to accuracy degradation and unstable optimization.
en.method: Introduces a State-Aware Adapter (SAA) to inject lightweight task-conditioned control signals into selective state-space updates for state-level adaptation, and Dual-Serialization Consistency Distillation (DSCD) to regularize different point cloud serializations.
en.result: Mantis achieves competitive performance across multiple benchmarks with only about 5% trainable parameters.
en.help_to_user: If you need to efficiently fine-tune 3D point cloud Mamba models, Mantis provides a plug-and-play state-level adaptation solution, significantly reducing storage and computation costs.

idea_spark.idea: Extend Dual-Serialization Consistency Distillation to multi-view or multi-modal consistency regularization to further improve generalization of Mamba backbones on non-sequential data.
idea_spark.risk: State-level adaptation may introduce additional computational overhead for long sequences or high-dimensional state spaces, requiring a trade-off between efficiency and performance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging the state-space dynamics of Mamba to design finer-grained adaptation mechanisms inspires fine-tuning methods for other sequence models (e.g., RWKV).

papers.title: Geometry over Density: Few-Shot Cross-Domain OOD Detection
papers.summary: Out-of-distribution (OOD) detection identifies test samples that fall outside a model's training distribution, a capability critical for safe deployment in high-stakes applications. Standard OOD detectors are trained on a specific in-distribution (ID) dataset and detect deviations from that single domain. In contrast, we study few-shot cross-domain OOD detection: given a \emph{single} pre-trained model, can we perform OOD detection on \emph{arbitrary} new ID-OOD task pairs using only a handful of ID samples at inference time, with no additional training? We propose \textbf{UFCOD}, a unified framework that achieves this goal through information-geometric analysis of diffusion trajectories. Our key insight is that diffusion noise predictions are score functions (gradients of log-density), and we extract two energy features: \emph{Path Energy} (integrated score magnitude) and \emph{Dynamics Energy} (score smoothness), that form a discrete Sobolev norm capturing how samples interact with the learned diffusion process. The central contribution is a \textbf{train-once, deploy-anywhere} paradigm: a diffusion model trained on a single dataset (e.g., CelebA) serves as a universal feature extractor for OOD detection across semantically unrelated domains (e.g., CIFAR-10, SVHN, Textures). At deployment, each new task requires only $\sim$100 unlabeled ID samples for inference: no retraining, no fine-tuning, no task-specific adaptation. Using 100 ID samples per task, UFCOD achieves 93.7\% average AUROC across 12 cross-domain benchmarks, competitive with methods trained on 50k--163k samples, demonstrating $\sim$500$\times$ improvement in sample efficiency. See our code in https://github.com/lili0415/UFCOD.

ai.tldr: 提出UFCOD框架，利用扩散轨迹的信息几何分析，实现仅需少量样本的跨域OOD检测。
ai.motivation: 现有OOD检测方法需针对特定域训练，无法灵活应对任意新域对。
ai.method: 从扩散噪声预测中提取路径能量和动力学能量，构成离散Sobolev范数作为特征。
ai.result: 仅用100个ID样本，在12个跨域基准上平均AUROC达93.7%，样本效率提升约500倍。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速检测模型在未知域上的异常样本，无需重新训练。

idea_spark.idea: 利用扩散轨迹的几何性质构造能量特征，实现零微调跨域检测。
idea_spark.risk: 对扩散模型质量敏感，低质量扩散模型可能影响特征有效性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从信息几何角度重新理解扩散过程，启发新的特征设计范式。

en.tldr: Proposes UFCOD, leveraging information-geometric analysis of diffusion trajectories for few-shot cross-domain OOD detection.
en.motivation: Existing OOD detectors are domain-specific and cannot handle arbitrary new ID-OOD pairs without retraining.
en.method: Extracts Path Energy and Dynamics Energy from diffusion noise predictions, forming a discrete Sobolev norm as features.
en.result: Achieves 93.7% average AUROC across 12 cross-domain benchmarks using only 100 ID samples, ~500x sample efficiency improvement.
en.help_to_user: Enables users to detect OOD samples in unseen domains without model retraining or fine-tuning.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage geometric properties of diffusion trajectories to construct energy features for zero-finetuning cross-domain detection.
idea_spark.risk: Sensitive to diffusion model quality; poor diffusion models may degrade feature effectiveness.
idea_spark.inspiration: Reinterpreting diffusion processes through information geometry inspires new feature design paradigms.

papers.title: An alternative formulation of the discrete-time fractional Poisson process
papers.summary: This paper introduces a discrete-time fractional Poisson process defined as a renewal process, where the waiting times follow a discrete Mittag-Leffler distribution. We investigate its fundamental properties by explicitly deriving the probability generating function of the waiting times and the exact probability distribution of the event counts. Through this analysis, we reveal that, unlike its continuous-time counterpart, our renewal-based model is not mathematically equivalent to the process constructed via subordination using the Sibuya distribution.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一种离散时间分数阶泊松过程，定义为更新过程，其中等待时间服从离散Mittag-Leffler分布，并推导了其基本性质。
ai.motivation: 连续时间分数阶泊松过程已有广泛研究，但其离散时间版本的定义和性质尚不明确，需要建立严格的数学框架。
ai.method: 采用更新过程框架，定义等待时间为离散Mittag-Leffler分布，推导概率生成函数和事件计数精确分布。
ai.result: 该更新模型与通过Sibuya分布次构造的过程在数学上不等价，揭示了离散与连续情形的重要差异。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解离散时间分数阶泊松过程的数学结构及其与连续版本的区别。

idea_spark.idea: 探索该过程在排队论或网络流量建模中的应用，利用其长记忆特性。
idea_spark.risk: 离散时间框架可能限制某些连续时间下的解析技巧，需开发新的数值方法。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从离散时间更新过程角度重新审视分数阶泊松过程，可能启发其他分数阶过程的离散化方案。

en.tldr: This paper introduces a discrete-time fractional Poisson process defined as a renewal process with discrete Mittag-Leffler waiting times, deriving its fundamental properties.
en.motivation: The continuous-time fractional Poisson process is well-studied, but its discrete-time counterpart lacks a rigorous definition and characterization.
en.method: Using a renewal process framework, waiting times follow a discrete Mittag-Leffler distribution; the probability generating function and exact event count distribution are derived.
en.result: This renewal-based model is not mathematically equivalent to the process constructed via subordination with the Sibuya distribution, highlighting key differences from the continuous case.
en.help_to_user: Provides insight into the mathematical structure of discrete-time fractional Poisson processes and their distinction from continuous versions.

idea_spark.idea: Explore applications in queueing theory or network traffic modeling leveraging long-memory properties.
idea_spark.risk: Discrete-time setting may limit analytical techniques from continuous time; new numerical methods may be needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Revisiting fractional Poisson processes from a discrete-time renewal perspective may inspire discretization schemes for other fractional processes.

papers.title: Automated Large-scale CVRP Solver Design via LLM-assisted Flexible MCTS
papers.summary: Solving large-scale CVRP (LSCVRP) with hundreds to thousands of nodes remains difficult for even state-of-the-art solvers. Divide-and-conquer can scale by decomposing the instance into size-reduced subproblems, but designing decomposition logic and configuring sub-solvers is highly expertise- and labor-intensive. Large Language Models (LLMs) have emerged as promising tools for automated algorithm design. However, existing LLM-driven approaches struggle with LSCVRP primarily due to the difficulty in generating sophisticated search strategies within a limited context window. To bridge this gap, we propose the LLM-assisted Flexible Monte Carlo Tree Search (LaF-MCTS), a novel framework that automates the design of high-performance LSCVRP solvers. We develop a three-tier decision hierarchy to enable incremental design of decomposition policies and sub-solvers for LSCVRP. To enable efficient search within the algorithmic hypothesis space, we introduce semantic pruning to eliminate semantically and structurally redundant codes, and branch regrowth to regenerate codes and preserve diversity. Extensive experiments on CVRPLib demonstrate that LaF-MCTS autonomously composes and optimizes decomposition-enhanced solvers that surpasses various state-of-the-art CVRP solvers.

ai.tldr: 提出LaF-MCTS框架，利用大语言模型辅助的灵活蒙特卡洛树搜索自动设计大规模CVRP求解器，通过三层决策层次、语义剪枝和分支再生，在CVRPLib上超越多种现有求解器。
ai.motivation: 大规模CVRP（数百至数千节点）难以求解，现有分治方法依赖专家手工设计分解逻辑和子求解器，而LLM驱动方法受限于上下文窗口难以生成复杂搜索策略。
ai.method: 构建三层决策层次逐步设计分解策略和子求解器；引入语义剪枝消除冗余代码，采用分支再生保持多样性，在算法假设空间内高效搜索。
ai.result: 在CVRPLib上，LaF-MCTS自动组合并优化分解增强求解器，性能超越多种最先进CVRP求解器。
ai.help_to_user: 为大规模组合优化问题提供自动化求解器设计方法，降低人工设计成本，提升求解性能。

idea_spark.idea: 将LLM与MCTS结合，通过语义剪枝和分支再生在算法空间高效搜索，自动生成高性能分解策略。
idea_spark.risk: LLM生成代码可能包含错误或低效逻辑，需验证；搜索空间过大时计算成本可能较高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用LLM的代码生成能力与MCTS的探索能力，自动设计复杂算法组件，减少人工干预。

en.tldr: Proposes LaF-MCTS, a framework that uses LLM-assisted flexible Monte Carlo tree search to automatically design large-scale CVRP solvers, with a three-tier decision hierarchy, semantic pruning, and branch regrowth, outperforming state-of-the-art solvers on CVRPLib.
en.motivation: Large-scale CVRP (hundreds to thousands of nodes) remains difficult; existing divide-and-conquer methods require expert manual design, and LLM-driven approaches struggle due to limited context windows.
en.method: Builds a three-tier decision hierarchy to incrementally design decomposition policies and sub-solvers; introduces semantic pruning to eliminate redundant code and branch regrowth to preserve diversity, enabling efficient search in the algorithmic hypothesis space.
en.result: On CVRPLib, LaF-MCTS autonomously composes and optimizes decomposition-enhanced solvers that surpass various state-of-the-art CVRP solvers.
en.help_to_user: Provides an automated solver design method for large-scale combinatorial optimization, reducing manual effort and improving performance.

idea_spark.idea: Combines LLM with MCTS, using semantic pruning and branch regrowth to efficiently search the algorithm space and automatically generate high-performance decomposition strategies.
idea_spark.risk: LLM-generated code may contain errors or inefficiencies; high computational cost when search space is large.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage LLM's code generation and MCTS's exploration to automatically design complex algorithmic components, reducing human intervention.

papers.title: Complex Analysis of Channel Polarization on discrete BMS Channels
papers.summary: We develop component evolution (CE), a complex-analytic framework for finite-blocklength channel polarization on discrete binary-input memoryless output-symmetric (BMS) channels. In this view, the Bhattacharyya parameter is treated as a real-valued instance of a broader class of complex-valued channel functionals. CE systematically derives analytic expressions for the Bhattacharyya parameters of the bit-channels of a given discrete BMS channel at arbitrary polarization levels. CE also enables structural analysis, providing new evidence of extremality of the binary erasure channel (BEC) and binary symmetric channel (BSC) through the lens of complex analysis, and revealing new channel-dependent recursions for a class of BSC bit-channels.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种复分析框架（组件演化），用于离散BMS信道的有限块长极化分析，并揭示了BEC和BSC的极值性。
ai.motivation: 现有极化分析多限于实值参数，缺乏对离散BMS信道有限块长下极化行为的系统复分析工具。
ai.method: 将Bhattacharyya参数视为复值信道泛函的实值实例，通过组件演化框架推导任意极化水平下比特信道的解析表达式。
ai.result: 建立了极化参数的解析表达式，证明了BEC和BSC的极值性，并发现了BSC比特信道的新递归关系。
ai.help_to_user: 为信道极化理论提供了复分析视角，有助于设计更高效的极化码和信道容量分析。

idea_spark.idea: 利用复分析研究极化码的有限块长性能，可能发现新的极化结构。
idea_spark.risk: 复分析工具可能增加计算复杂度，且对非对称信道的适用性待验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将实值参数扩展到复域，为信道极化研究开辟新方向。

idea_spark.inspiration: 将实值参数扩展到复域，为信道极化研究开辟新方向。

en.tldr: Proposes a complex-analytic framework (component evolution) for finite-blocklength channel polarization on discrete BMS channels, revealing extremality of BEC and BSC.
en.motivation: Existing polarization analysis is mostly limited to real-valued parameters, lacking a systematic complex-analytic tool for finite-blocklength behavior on discrete BMS channels.
en.method: Treats the Bhattacharyya parameter as a real-valued instance of complex-valued channel functionals, deriving analytic expressions for bit-channel Bhattacharyya parameters at arbitrary polarization levels via component evolution.
en.result: Establishes analytic expressions for polarization parameters, proves extremality of BEC and BSC, and reveals new recursions for a class of BSC bit-channels.
en.help_to_user: Provides a complex-analytic perspective on channel polarization, aiding in the design of more efficient polar codes and channel capacity analysis.

idea_spark.idea: Using complex analysis to study finite-blocklength performance of polar codes may uncover new polarization structures.
idea_spark.risk: Complex-analytic tools may increase computational complexity, and applicability to non-symmetric channels remains to be verified.
idea_spark.inspiration: Extending real-valued parameters to the complex domain opens new directions for channel polarization research.

papers.title: Pareto-type finite-block optimality for source codes: a constrained Markov example
papers.summary: We study a Pareto-type notion of finite-block optimality for injective source codes, where two codes are compared through the full sequence of expected block lengths. As a concrete and fully analyzable test case, we revisit the four-symbol constrained Markov source introduced by Dalai and Leonardi in their "meaningful example'' on constrained-source decodability. For each admissible nonempty string $u=x_1^m \in \mathscr{A} \subset \mathscr{X}^+$, let $$ K(u):=-\log_2 \mathbb{P}(X_1^m=u) $$ denote its information cost. We construct a canonical injective binary mapping $C:\mathscr{A} \to \{0,1\}^+$ by ordering admissible strings by increasing $K(u)$, then by length and lexicographic order, and assigning binary strings in shortlex order. For the length-$n$ block $X_1^n$ we prove $$ \mathbb{E}[|C(X_1)|]=\tfrac32, \qquad \mathbb{E}[|C(X_1^n)|]<\tfrac32\,n\quad (n\ge 2). $$ Moreover, for every fixed $$ 0<c<\frac{\sqrt2}{18\sqrtπ} $$ we have $$ \mathbb{E}[|C(X_1^n)|]\le \tfrac32\,n-\frac{c}{\sqrt n} $$ for all sufficiently large $n$. Thus, for this source, the reversible Dalai-Leonardi code is not Pareto-optimal with respect to finite-block average length. The proof is based on an exact enumeration of admissible strings by information cost and on a shortlex gap identity implying that each cost class splits evenly between lengths $K(u)-1$ and $K(u)$. The example is simple, but it already exhibits the kind of finite-block Pareto comparison that seems natural for injective source coding under source constraints.

ai.tldr: 本文研究注入式源编码的帕累托型有限块最优性，以Dalai-Leonardi约束马尔可夫源为例，构造规范二元映射并证明其平均块长不满足帕累托最优。
ai.motivation: 探索有限块长度下源编码的帕累托最优性，为约束源编码提供理论分析框架。
ai.method: 通过信息代价排序、短字典序映射和精确枚举可容许字符串，推导期望块长上界。
ai.result: 对于该约束马尔可夫源，可逆Dalai-Leonardi码在有限块平均长度意义下不是帕累托最优的。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解有限块编码中的帕累托比较方法及其在约束源上的应用。

idea_spark.idea: 利用信息代价分类和短字典序间隙恒等式构造更优编码。
idea_spark.risk: 分析依赖于特定源结构，通用性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发在有限块编码中考虑多目标优化（如平均长度与方差）。

zh.tldr: 本文研究注入式源编码的帕累托型有限块最优性，以Dalai-Leonardi约束马尔可夫源为例，构造规范二元映射并证明其平均块长不满足帕累托最优。
zh.motivation: 探索有限块长度下源编码的帕累托最优性，为约束源编码提供理论分析框架。
zh.method: 通过信息代价排序、短字典序映射和精确枚举可容许字符串，推导期望块长上界。
zh.result: 对于该约束马尔可夫源，可逆Dalai-Leonardi码在有限块平均长度意义下不是帕累托最优的。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解有限块编码中的帕累托比较方法及其在约束源上的应用。

idea_spark.idea: 利用信息代价分类和短字典序间隙恒等式构造更优编码。
idea_spark.risk: 分析依赖于特定源结构，通用性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发在有限块编码中考虑多目标优化（如平均长度与方差）。

en.tldr: This paper studies Pareto-type finite-block optimality for injective source codes, using a constrained Markov source as a test case, and shows that the reversible Dalai-Leonardi code is not Pareto-optimal in terms of finite-block average length.
en.motivation: To explore Pareto optimality in finite-block source coding and provide a theoretical framework for constrained sources.
en.method: Construct a canonical binary mapping via information cost ordering and shortlex order, and derive upper bounds on expected block lengths through exact enumeration.
en.result: For the constrained Markov source, the reversible Dalai-Leonardi code is not Pareto-optimal with respect to finite-block average length.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand Pareto comparisons in finite-block coding and their application to constrained sources.

idea_spark.idea: Use information cost classification and shortlex gap identity to construct better codes.
idea_spark.risk: The analysis depends on the specific source structure; generality needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires multi-objective optimization (e.g., average length and variance) in finite-block coding.

papers.title: UniCorrn: Unified Correspondence Transformer Across 2D and 3D
papers.summary: Visual correspondence across image-to-image (2D-2D), image-to-point cloud (2D-3D), and point cloud-to-point cloud (3D-3D) geometric matching forms the foundation for numerous 3D vision tasks. Despite sharing a similar problem structure, current methods use task-specific designs with separate models for each modality combination. We present UniCorrn, the first correspondence model with shared weights that unifies geometric matching across all three tasks. Our key insight is that Transformer attention naturally captures cross-modal feature similarity. We propose a dual-stream decoder that maintains separate appearance and positional feature streams. This design enables end-to-end learning through stack-able layers while supporting flexible query-based correspondence estimation across heterogeneous modalities. Our architecture employs modality-specific backbones followed by shared encoder and decoder components, trained jointly on diverse data combining pseudo point clouds from depth maps with real 3D correspondence annotations. UniCorrn achieves competitive performance on 2D-2D matching and surpasses prior state-of-the-art by 8% on 7Scenes (2D-3D) and 10% on 3DLoMatch (3D-3D) in registration recall. Project website: https://neu-vi.github.io/UniCorrn

ai.tldr: 提出UniCorrn，首个统一2D-2D、2D-3D和3D-3D几何匹配的共享权重对应模型。
ai.motivation: 现有方法针对不同模态组合使用独立模型，缺乏统一框架，效率低且泛化性差。
ai.method: 采用模态特定骨干网络后接共享编码器和解码器，解码器包含双流结构（外观流和位置流），通过Transformer注意力捕获跨模态特征相似性，支持端到端学习。
ai.result: 在2D-2D匹配上达到竞争性能，在7Scenes（2D-3D）和3DLoMatch（3D-3D）上分别提升8%和10%的配准召回率。
ai.help_to_user: 提供统一的几何匹配解决方案，简化多模态3D视觉任务的模型部署。

idea_spark.idea: 利用共享权重和Transformer注意力统一异构模态的对应学习，减少模型数量。
idea_spark.risk: 伪点云与真实点云分布差异可能影响泛化，需更多真实3D标注数据。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从自然语言处理中的多任务学习获得启发，将不同模态匹配视为统一问题。

en.tldr: UniCorrn is the first unified correspondence model with shared weights for 2D-2D, 2D-3D, and 3D-3D geometric matching.
en.motivation: Existing methods use separate models for each modality combination, lacking a unified framework, which is inefficient and generalizes poorly.
en.method: It uses modality-specific backbones followed by shared encoder and decoder. The decoder has a dual-stream design (appearance and positional streams) leveraging Transformer attention to capture cross-modal feature similarity, enabling end-to-end learning.
en.result: Achieves competitive performance on 2D-2D matching and surpasses prior state-of-the-art by 8% on 7Scenes (2D-3D) and 10% on 3DLoMatch (3D-3D) in registration recall.
en.help_to_user: Provides a unified geometric matching solution, simplifying model deployment for multi-modal 3D vision tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Use shared weights and Transformer attention to unify correspondence learning across heterogeneous modalities, reducing the number of models.
idea_spark.risk: Distribution mismatch between pseudo point clouds and real point clouds may affect generalization; more real 3D annotations are needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by multi-task learning in NLP, treating different modality matching as a unified problem.

papers.title: PriorNet: Prior-Guided Engagement Estimation from Face Video
papers.summary: Engagement estimation from face video remains challenging because facial evidence is often incomplete, labeled data are limited, and engagement annotations are subjective. We present PriorNet, a prior-guided framework that injects task-relevant priors at three stages of the pipeline: preprocessing, model adaptation, and objective design. PriorNet converts face-detection failures into explicit zero-frame placeholders so that missing-face events remain represented in the input sequence, adapts a frozen Self-supervised Video Facial Affect Perceiver (SVFAP) backbone through a Prior-guided Low-Rank Adaptation module (Prior-LoRA) for parameter-efficient specialization, and trains with a Dirichlet-evidential, uncertainty-weighted objective under hard-label supervision. We evaluate PriorNet on EngageNet, DAiSEE, DREAMS, and PAFE using each dataset's native evaluation protocol. Across these benchmarks, PriorNet improves over the strongest listed prior reference within each dataset's evaluation framing, while component ablations on EngageNet and DAiSEE indicate that the gains arise from complementary contributions of preprocessing, adaptation, and objective-level priors. These results support explicit prior injection as a useful design principle for face-video engagement estimation under the benchmark conditions studied in this work.

ai.tldr: 提出PriorNet，通过预处理、模型适配和损失设计三阶段注入先验知识，提升面部视频中的参与度估计性能。
ai.motivation: 面部视频参与度估计面临面部证据不完整、标注数据有限和标注主观性强等挑战。
ai.method: 将检测失败帧显式填充为零占位符；使用Prior-LoRA模块冻结SVFAP骨干进行参数高效适配；采用Dirichlet证据不确定性加权损失进行训练。
ai.result: 在EngageNet、DAiSEE、DREAMS和PAFE四个数据集上均优于现有最强基线，消融实验验证了三阶段先验的互补贡献。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种系统性的先验注入设计原则，可应用于其他面部视频分析任务。

idea_spark.idea: 将缺失帧显式建模为占位符，结合参数高效微调与不确定性加权损失，形成统一先验框架。
idea_spark.risk: 先验设计依赖任务领域知识，可能不适用于先验不明确的场景。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受缺失数据处理和贝叶斯深度学习启发，将先验知识系统性地融入模型各阶段。

en.tldr: Proposes PriorNet, a prior-guided framework that injects task-relevant priors at preprocessing, model adaptation, and objective design stages to improve engagement estimation from face video.
en.motivation: Engagement estimation from face video is challenging due to incomplete facial evidence, limited labeled data, and subjective annotations.
en.method: Converts face-detection failures into explicit zero-frame placeholders; adapts a frozen SVFAP backbone via Prior-LoRA for parameter-efficient specialization; trains with a Dirichlet-evidential, uncertainty-weighted objective under hard-label supervision.
en.result: Outperforms the strongest baselines on EngageNet, DAiSEE, DREAMS, and PAFE; ablation studies confirm complementary contributions of the three-stage priors.
en.help_to_user: Provides a systematic prior injection design principle applicable to other face-video analysis tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Explicitly modeling missing frames as placeholders, combined with parameter-efficient fine-tuning and uncertainty-weighted loss, forms a unified prior framework.
idea_spark.risk: Prior design relies on domain knowledge and may not generalize to tasks with unclear priors.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by missing data handling and Bayesian deep learning, systematically integrating prior knowledge into all stages of the model pipeline.

papers.title: Stream-R1: Reliability-Perplexity Aware Reward Distillation for Streaming Video Generation
papers.summary: Distillation-based acceleration has become foundational for making autoregressive streaming video diffusion models practical, with distribution matching distillation (DMD) as the de facto choice. Existing methods, however, train the student to match the teacher's output indiscriminately, treating every rollout, frame, and pixel as equally reliable supervision. We argue that this caps distilled quality, since it overlooks two complementary axes of variance in DMD supervision: Inter-Reliability across student rollouts whose supervision varies in reliability, and Intra-Perplexity across spatial regions and temporal frames that contribute unequally to where quality can still be improved. The objective thus conflates two questions under a uniform weight: whether to learn from each rollout, and where to concentrate optimization within it. To address this, we propose Stream-R1, a Reliability-Perplexity Aware Reward Distillation framework that adaptively reweights the distillation objective at both rollout and spatiotemporal-element levels through a single shared reward-guided mechanism. At the Inter-Reliability level, Stream-R1 rescales each rollout's loss by an exponential of a pretrained video reward score, so that rollouts with reliable supervision dominate optimization. At the Intra-Perplexity level, it back-propagates the same reward model to extract per-pixel gradient saliency, which is factored into spatial and temporal weights that concentrate optimization pressure on regions and frames where refinement yields the largest expected gain. An adaptive balancing mechanism prevents any single quality axis from dominating across visual quality, motion quality, and text alignment. Stream-R1 attains consistent improvements on all three dimensions over distillation baselines on standard streaming video generation benchmarks, without architectural modification or additional inference cost.

ai.tldr: 提出Stream-R1框架，通过奖励引导的自适应重加权蒸馏目标，提升流式视频生成质量。
ai.motivation: 现有蒸馏方法对所有学生展开、帧和像素一视同仁，忽略了监督信号在展开间可靠性和空间时间区域困惑度上的差异，限制了蒸馏质量。
ai.method: 在展开级用视频奖励分数的指数重缩放损失，在时空元素级用奖励模型反向传播的梯度显著性提取权重，并加入自适应平衡机制。
ai.result: 在标准流式视频生成基准上，视觉质量、运动质量和文本对齐三个维度均优于蒸馏基线，且无需修改架构或增加推理成本。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过奖励感知的蒸馏策略提升流式视频生成模型的效率和质量。

idea_spark.idea: 利用预训练奖励模型的梯度显著性作为空间时间注意力权重，动态聚焦优化区域。
idea_spark.risk: 奖励模型的质量和泛化性可能影响权重分配的准确性，需谨慎选择或微调。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从强化学习中的奖励塑形和注意力机制中获得启发，将奖励信号同时用于样本级和元素级重加权。

zh.result: 在标准流式视频生成基准上，视觉质量、运动质量和文本对齐三个维度均优于蒸馏基线，且无需修改架构或增加推理成本。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过奖励感知的蒸馏策略提升流式视频生成模型的效率和质量。

idea_spark.risk: 奖励模型的质量和泛化性可能影响权重分配的准确性，需谨慎选择或微调。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从强化学习中的奖励塑形和注意力机制中获得启发，将奖励信号同时用于样本级和元素级重加权。

en.tldr: Proposes Stream-R1, a reward-guided adaptive reweighting distillation framework for streaming video generation, improving quality across visual, motion, and text alignment axes.
en.motivation: Existing distillation methods treat all rollouts, frames, and pixels equally, ignoring inter-rollout reliability and intra-frame perplexity variations, which caps distilled quality.
en.method: Reweights distillation loss at rollout level via exponential of video reward score, and at spatiotemporal level via gradient saliency from the same reward model, with an adaptive balancing mechanism.
en.result: Achieves consistent improvements over distillation baselines on standard streaming video generation benchmarks without architectural changes or extra inference cost.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how reward-aware distillation can enhance efficiency and quality in streaming video generation models.

idea_spark.idea: Use gradient saliency from a pretrained reward model as spatiotemporal attention weights to dynamically focus optimization.
idea_spark.risk: Quality and generalization of the reward model may affect weight assignment accuracy; careful selection or fine-tuning is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by reward shaping in reinforcement learning and attention mechanisms, using reward signals for both sample-level and element-level reweighting.

papers.title: Real-Time Evaluation of Autonomous Systems under Adversarial Attacks
papers.summary: Most evaluations of autonomous driving policies under adversarial conditions are conducted in simulation, due to cost efficiency and the absence of physical risk. However, purely virtual testing fails to capture structural inconsistencies, supervision constraints, and state-representation effects that arise in real-world data and fundamentally shape policy robustness. This work presents an offline trajectory-learning and adversarial robustness evaluation framework grounded in real-world intersection driving data. Within a controlled data contract, we train and compare three trajectory-learning paradigms: Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP)-based Behavior Cloning (BC), Transformer-based object-tokenized BC, and inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) formulated within a Generative Adversarial Imitation Learning (GAIL) framework. Models are evaluated using Average Displacement Error (ADE) and Final Displacement Error (FDE).   Inference-time robustness is assessed by subjecting trained policies to gradient-based adversarial perturbations across multiple intersection scenarios, yielding a structured robustness evaluation matrix. Results show that state-structure design and architectural inductive biases critically influence adversarial stability, leading to markedly different robustness profiles despite comparable nominal prediction accuracy (ADE < 0.08). Inference-time Projected Gradient Descent (PGD) attacks induce final displacement errors of up to approximately 8 meters. The proposed framework establishes a scalable benchmark for studying offline trajectory learning and adversarial robustness in real-world autonomous driving settings.

ai.tldr: 提出一个基于真实世界交叉路口数据的离线轨迹学习与对抗鲁棒性评估框架，比较MLP、Transformer和GAIL三种范式，发现状态结构设计显著影响对抗稳定性。
ai.motivation: 现有自动驾驶对抗评估多在仿真中进行，无法捕捉真实数据中的结构不一致性、监督约束和状态表示效应。
ai.method: 在受控数据合约下，训练MLP行为克隆、Transformer行为克隆和GAIL逆强化学习模型，使用PGD攻击进行推理时鲁棒性评估。
ai.result: 尽管名义预测精度相近（ADE<0.08），不同模型在PGD攻击下最终位移误差可达约8米，状态结构设计对对抗稳定性至关重要。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解真实世界数据下不同轨迹学习范式的对抗鲁棒性差异，为安全部署提供基准。

idea_spark.idea: 结合状态结构设计与对抗训练，开发更鲁棒的轨迹预测模型。
idea_spark.risk: 真实数据合约可能限制泛化性，且PGD攻击假设白盒场景，实际黑盒攻击效果可能不同。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从真实数据中提取结构不一致性特征，设计更逼真的对抗攻击场景。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解真实世界数据下不同轨迹学习范式的对抗鲁棒性差异，为安全部署提供基准。

idea_spark.idea: 结合状态结构设计与对抗训练，开发更鲁棒的轨迹预测模型。
idea_spark.risk: 真实数据合约可能限制泛化性，且PGD攻击假设白盒场景，实际黑盒攻击效果可能不同。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从真实数据中提取结构不一致性特征，设计更逼真的对抗攻击场景。

en.tldr: Proposes an offline trajectory-learning and adversarial robustness evaluation framework using real-world intersection data, comparing MLP, Transformer, and GAIL paradigms, showing state-structure design critically influences adversarial stability.
en.motivation: Most adversarial evaluations for autonomous driving are simulated, failing to capture structural inconsistencies, supervision constraints, and state-representation effects from real-world data.
en.method: Under a controlled data contract, train MLP behavior cloning, Transformer behavior cloning, and GAIL inverse reinforcement learning models, then evaluate inference-time robustness via PGD attacks.
en.result: Despite comparable nominal accuracy (ADE<0.08), models exhibit final displacement errors up to ~8m under PGD attacks, with state-structure design critically affecting adversarial stability.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand adversarial robustness differences among trajectory-learning paradigms in real-world data, providing a benchmark for safe deployment.

idea_spark.idea: Combine state-structure design with adversarial training to develop more robust trajectory prediction models.
idea_spark.risk: Real-world data contracts may limit generalizability, and PGD attacks assume white-box scenarios; black-box attacks may yield different results.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage structural inconsistencies from real data to design more realistic adversarial attack scenarios.

papers.title: RD-ViT: Recurrent-Depth Vision Transformer for Semantic Segmentation with Reduced Data Dependence Extending the Recurrent-Depth Transformer Architecture to Dense Prediction
papers.summary: Vision Transformers (ViTs) achieve state-of-the-art segmentation accuracy but require large training datasets because each layer has unique parameters that must be learned independently. We present RD-ViT, a Recurrent-Depth Vision Transformer that adapts the Recurrent-Depth Transformer (RDT) architecture to dense prediction tasks, supporting both 2D and 3D inputs. RD-ViT replaces the deep stack of unique transformer blocks with a single shared block looped T times, augmented with LTI-stable state injection for guaranteed convergence, Adaptive Computation Time (ACT) for spatial compute allocation, depth-wise LoRA adaptation, and optional Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) feed-forward networks for category-specific specialization. We evaluate on the ACDC cardiac MRI segmentation benchmark in both 2D slice-level and 3D volumetric settings with exclusively real experiments executed in Google Colab. In 2D, RD-ViT outperforms standard ViT at 10% training data (Dice 0.774 vs 0.762) and at full data (0.882 vs 0.872). In 3D, RD-ViT with MoE achieves Dice 0.812 with 3.0M parameters, reaching 99.4% of standard ViT performance (0.817) at 53% of the parameter count. MoE expert utilization analysis reveals that different experts spontaneously specialize for different cardiac structures (RV, MYO, LV) without explicit routing supervision. ACT halting maps show higher compute allocation at cardiac boundaries, and the mean ponder time decreases from 2.6 to 1.4 iterations during training, demonstrating learned computational efficiency. Depth extrapolation enables inference with more loops than training without degradation. All code, notebooks, and results are publicly released.

ai.tldr: 提出RD-ViT，通过循环深度变换器减少语义分割对大数据集的依赖，在ACDC心脏MRI分割任务上以更少参数达到或超越标准ViT性能。
ai.motivation: 标准ViT每层独立参数导致训练数据需求大，限制其在数据稀缺场景的应用。
ai.method: 将深度堆叠的独立Transformer块替换为单个共享块循环T次，结合LTI稳定状态注入、自适应计算时间、深度LoRA和可选MoE前馈网络。
ai.result: 2D下10%数据Dice 0.774 vs 0.762，全数据0.882 vs 0.872；3D下MoE版3.0M参数达标准ViT 99.4%性能；专家自动分工于不同心脏结构。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种数据高效的分割架构，适合标注稀缺的医学影像场景，并公开代码和实验细节。

idea_spark.idea: 结合ACT和MoE实现动态计算分配与类别特化，可启发自适应推理系统。
idea_spark.risk: 循环共享块可能限制模型容量，在极复杂场景下性能提升有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从循环神经网络和Transformer融合中获取灵感，探索参数共享与深度扩展的平衡。

zh.motivation: 标准ViT每层独立参数导致训练数据需求大，限制其在数据稀缺场景的应用。

zh.help_to_user: 提供一种数据高效的分割架构，适合标注稀缺的医学影像场景，并公开代码和实验细节。

idea_spark.idea: 结合ACT和MoE实现动态计算分配与类别特化，可启发自适应推理系统。
idea_spark.risk: 循环共享块可能限制模型容量，在极复杂场景下性能提升有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从循环神经网络和Transformer融合中获取灵感，探索参数共享与深度扩展的平衡。

en.tldr: RD-ViT adapts the Recurrent-Depth Transformer to dense prediction, reducing data dependence for semantic segmentation and achieving competitive results on cardiac MRI with fewer parameters.
en.motivation: Standard ViTs require large training datasets due to independent parameters per layer, limiting applicability in data-scarce domains.
en.method: Replaces deep stack of unique transformer blocks with a single shared block looped T times, augmented with LTI-stable state injection, Adaptive Computation Time, depth-wise LoRA, and optional MoE.
en.result: 2D: Dice 0.774 vs 0.762 at 10% data, 0.882 vs 0.872 at full data; 3D: MoE version with 3.0M params reaches 99.4% of standard ViT performance; experts specialize for different cardiac structures.
en.help_to_user: Offers a data-efficient segmentation architecture suitable for medical imaging with limited annotations, with publicly released code and notebooks.

idea_spark.idea: Combining ACT and MoE for dynamic compute allocation and category specialization could inspire adaptive inference systems.
idea_spark.risk: Shared recurrent block may limit model capacity, potentially underperforming on extremely complex scenes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws inspiration from fusion of RNN and Transformer, exploring trade-offs between parameter sharing and depth scaling.

papers.title: FUS3DMaps: Scalable and Accurate Open-Vocabulary Semantic Mapping by 3D Fusion of Voxel- and Instance-Level Layers
papers.summary: Open-vocabulary semantic mapping enables robots to spatially ground previously unseen concepts without requiring predefined class sets. Current training-free methods commonly rely on multi-view fusion of semantic embeddings into a 3D map, either at the instance-level via segmenting views and encoding image crops of segments, or by projecting image patch embeddings directly into a dense semantic map. The latter approach sidesteps segmentation and 2D-to-3D instance association by operating on full uncropped image frames, but existing methods remain limited in scalability. We present FUS3DMaps, an online dual-layer semantic mapping method that jointly maintains both dense and instance-level open-vocabulary layers within a shared voxel map. This design enables further voxel-level semantic fusion of the layer embeddings, combining the complementary strengths of both semantic mapping approaches. We find that our proposed semantic cross-layer fusion approach improves the quality of both the instance-level and dense layers, while also enabling a scalable and highly accurate instance-level map where the dense layer and cross-layer fusion are restricted to a spatial sliding window. Experiments on established 3D semantic segmentation benchmarks as well as a selection of large-scale scenes show that FUS3DMaps achieves accurate open-vocabulary semantic mapping at multi-story building scales. Additional material and code will be made available: https://githanonymous.github.io/FUS3DMaps/.

ai.tldr: 提出FUS3DMaps，一种在线双层次语义映射方法，通过共享体素地图中的密集层和实例层融合，实现可扩展且准确的开词汇语义映射。
ai.motivation: 现有开词汇语义映射方法在可扩展性上受限，且密集层与实例层方法各有优劣，缺乏有效融合。
ai.method: 维护共享体素地图中的密集层和实例层，通过跨层语义融合结合两者优势，并在滑动窗口内限制密集层和融合操作以提升可扩展性。
ai.result: 在3D语义分割基准和大规模场景上，FUS3DMaps实现了多楼层建筑尺度的准确开词汇语义映射。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过双层次融合实现可扩展且准确的开词汇语义地图构建。

idea_spark.idea: 利用滑动窗口限制密集层融合，在保持精度的同时大幅提升大规模场景的可扩展性。
idea_spark.risk: 滑动窗口大小和跨层融合权重需仔细调参，可能影响不同场景下的泛化性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受密集层和实例层方法互补性的启发，通过共享体素地图实现两者的协同优化。

zh.tldr: 提出FUS3DMaps，一种在线双层次语义映射方法，通过共享体素地图中的密集层和实例层融合，实现可扩展且准确的开词汇语义映射。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过双层次融合实现可扩展且准确的开词汇语义地图构建。

idea_spark.idea: 利用滑动窗口限制密集层融合，在保持精度的同时大幅提升大规模场景的可扩展性。
idea_spark.risk: 滑动窗口大小和跨层融合权重需仔细调参，可能影响不同场景下的泛化性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受密集层和实例层方法互补性的启发，通过共享体素地图实现两者的协同优化。

en.tldr: Proposes FUS3DMaps, an online dual-layer semantic mapping method that fuses dense and instance-level layers in a shared voxel map for scalable and accurate open-vocabulary semantic mapping.
en.motivation: Existing open-vocabulary semantic mapping methods are limited in scalability, and dense and instance-level approaches have complementary strengths but lack effective fusion.
en.method: Maintains both dense and instance-level layers in a shared voxel map, performs cross-layer semantic fusion to combine their strengths, and restricts dense layer and fusion to a sliding window for scalability.
en.result: Achieves accurate open-vocabulary semantic mapping at multi-story building scales on 3D semantic segmentation benchmarks and large-scale scenes.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how dual-layer fusion enables scalable and accurate open-vocabulary semantic mapping.

idea_spark.idea: Using a sliding window to restrict dense layer fusion significantly improves scalability for large-scale scenes while maintaining accuracy.
idea_spark.risk: The sliding window size and cross-layer fusion weights require careful tuning, potentially affecting generalization across different scenes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the complementary strengths of dense and instance-level methods, achieving synergistic optimization through a shared voxel map.

papers.title: Towards Open World Sound Event Detection
papers.summary: Sound Event Detection (SED) plays a vital role in audio understanding, with applications in surveillance, smart cities, healthcare, and multimedia indexing. However, conventional SED systems operate under a closed-world assumption, limiting their effectiveness in real-world environments where novel acoustic events frequently emerge. Inspired by the success of open-world learning in computer vision, we introduce the Open-World Sound Event Detection (OW-SED) paradigm, where models must detect known events, identify unseen ones, and incrementally learn from them. To tackle the unique challenges of OW-SED, such as overlapping and ambiguous events, we propose a 1D Deformable architecture that leverages deformable attention to adaptively focus on salient temporal regions. Furthermore, we design a novel Open-World Deformable Sound Event Detection Transformer (WOOT) framework incorporating feature disentanglement to separate class-specific and class-agnostic representations, together with a one-to-many matching strategy and a diversity loss to enhance representation diversity. Experimental results demonstrate that our method achieves marginally superior performance compared to existing leading techniques in closed-world settings and significantly improves over existing baselines in open-world scenarios.

ai.tldr: 提出开放世界声音事件检测范式，并设计WOOT框架，通过可变形注意力、特征解耦和多样性损失，在闭集和开集场景下均取得领先性能。
ai.motivation: 传统声音事件检测系统基于闭集假设，无法处理真实环境中不断出现的新声音事件。
ai.method: 采用1D可变形架构自适应关注显著时间区域；设计WOOT框架，包含特征解耦分离类别特定与类别无关表示、一对多匹配策略和多样性损失。
ai.result: 在闭集场景下性能略优于现有方法，在开集场景下显著超越基线。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解开放世界声音事件检测的核心挑战与解决方案，以及WOOT框架的设计思路。

idea_spark.idea: 将WOOT中的一对多匹配策略与多样性损失应用于多标签音频分类，提升模型对重叠事件的区分能力。
idea_spark.risk: 开集检测中未知事件的定义和标注可能引入噪声，影响模型增量学习的稳定性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴计算机视觉开放世界学习成功经验，将闭集检测扩展至开集场景，启发更多音频领域的开放世界研究。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解开放世界声音事件检测的核心挑战与解决方案，以及WOOT框架的设计思路。

idea_spark.risk: 开集检测中未知事件的定义和标注可能引入噪声，影响模型增量学习的稳定性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴计算机视觉开放世界学习成功经验，将闭集检测扩展至开集场景，启发更多音频领域的开放世界研究。

en.tldr: Proposes the Open-World Sound Event Detection paradigm and the WOOT framework, leveraging deformable attention, feature disentanglement, and diversity loss to achieve leading performance in both closed-world and open-world settings.
en.motivation: Conventional SED systems operate under a closed-world assumption, limiting their effectiveness in real-world environments where novel acoustic events frequently emerge.
en.method: Employs a 1D Deformable architecture to adaptively focus on salient temporal regions; designs the WOOT framework with feature disentanglement to separate class-specific and class-agnostic representations, a one-to-many matching strategy, and a diversity loss.
en.result: Achieves marginally superior performance in closed-world settings and significantly improves over baselines in open-world scenarios.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the core challenges and solutions of open-world sound event detection, as well as the design rationale of the WOOT framework.

idea_spark.idea: Apply the one-to-many matching strategy and diversity loss from WOOT to multi-label audio classification to improve discrimination of overlapping events.
idea_spark.risk: Definition and annotation of unknown events in open-world detection may introduce noise, affecting the stability of incremental learning.
idea_spark.inspiration: Drawing on successful open-world learning in computer vision inspires extending closed-set detection to open-world scenarios in audio domains.

papers.title: HackerSignal: A Large-Scale Multi-Source Dataset Linking Hacker Community Discourse to the CVE Vulnerability Lifecycle
papers.summary: We introduce HackerSignal, a benchmark for temporal out-of-distribution cyber threat intelligence (CTI) and cross-source CVE linkage. HackerSignal aggregates 7.45 million exact-deduplicated documents from 64 public forum/source identifiers spanning eight source layers and a 36-year window (1990-2026). In contrast to other publicly accessible cybersecurity datasets, HackerSignal is among the first public benchmark datasets that maps the full potential exploit to vulnerability trajectory from hacker community discourse, exploit databases with working and proof of concept exploits, vulnerability advisories, and software fix commits. HackerSignal creates these linkages through a shared CVE identifier space while preserving source-specific release modes to support a range of unique Artificial Intelligence (AI)-enabled cybersecurity analytics tasks. In this paper, we summarize HackerSignal and illustrate three selected benchmark tasks it uniquely supports: (1) CVE linkage retrieval (cross-source temporal out-of-distribution entity grounding); (2) exploit type classification (8-class vulnerability type prediction with temporal OOD evaluation); and (3) temporal generalization (prospective CVE-disjoint evaluation where C_train and C_test are disjoint). All tasks use temporal splits to evaluate prospective generalization. We release source-shortcut and leakage diagnostics, manual-audit packets, a datasheet, and a release-governance addendum to support the dissemination of the dataset. HackerSignal's code, data, and Croissant metadata are available at hf.co/datasets/BenAmpel/HackerSignal (data) and github.com/BenAmpel/hackersignal (code).

ai.tldr: 提出HackerSignal数据集，整合64个公开论坛/来源的745万文档，覆盖1990-2026年，支持CVE链接检索、漏洞类型分类和时间泛化等AI网络安全分析任务。
ai.motivation: 现有网络安全数据集缺乏跨来源、时间分布外的CVE关联能力，无法全面映射从黑客社区讨论到漏洞修复的完整轨迹。
ai.method: 从64个公共论坛/来源标识符收集文档，经精确去重后得到745万条记录，通过共享CVE标识符空间建立跨来源链接，并保留来源特定的发布模式。
ai.result: 构建了首个公开基准数据集，支持三种独特任务：CVE链接检索、漏洞类型分类（8类）和时间泛化评估，并提供诊断工具和治理文档。
ai.help_to_user: 可用于训练和评估AI模型在网络安全威胁情报中的时间外分布泛化能力，特别是跨来源CVE关联和漏洞类型预测。

idea_spark.idea: 利用该数据集训练模型，预测新漏洞出现前黑客社区中的早期预警信号。
idea_spark.risk: 数据来源可能包含噪声或虚假信息，需注意模型对来源偏差的过拟合。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合时间外分布评估，设计更鲁棒的跨来源实体链接算法。

zh.tldr: 提出HackerSignal数据集，整合64个公开论坛/来源的745万文档，覆盖1990-2026年，支持CVE链接检索、漏洞类型分类和时间泛化等AI网络安全分析任务。

en.tldr: Introduces HackerSignal, a dataset of 7.45M deduplicated documents from 64 public sources (1990-2026), enabling CVE linkage retrieval, exploit type classification, and temporal generalization for AI-driven cyber threat intelligence.
en.motivation: Existing cybersecurity datasets lack cross-source, temporal out-of-distribution CVE linkage, failing to map the full trajectory from hacker discourse to vulnerability fix.
en.method: Aggregates documents from 64 public forum/source identifiers, exact-deduplicates to 7.45M records, and links them via shared CVE identifiers while preserving source-specific release modes.
en.result: Constructs the first public benchmark supporting three tasks: CVE linkage retrieval, 8-class exploit type classification, and temporal generalization, with diagnostics and governance addenda.
en.help_to_user: Useful for training and evaluating AI models on temporal out-of-distribution generalization in cyber threat intelligence, especially cross-source CVE linking and vulnerability type prediction.

idea_spark.idea: Train models to predict early warning signals in hacker community discourse before a new vulnerability is disclosed.
idea_spark.risk: Data sources may contain noise or misinformation; models risk overfitting to source-specific biases.
idea_spark.inspiration: Design more robust cross-source entity linking algorithms leveraging temporal out-of-distribution evaluation.

papers.title: Identity-Consistent Multi-Pose Generation of Contactless Fingerprints
papers.summary: Contactless fingerprint recognition has gained increasing attention due to its advantages in hygiene and acquisition flexibility. However, the absence of physical contact constraints introduces severe nonlinear geometric distortions caused by free finger poses in 3D space, resulting in a substantial cross-modal domain gap between contactless and conventional contact-based fingerprints. Existing solutions largely rely on explicit geometric correction or image enhancement, which are fragile under extreme pose variations. In this paper, we propose Identity-Consistent Multi-Pose Generation of Contactless Fingerprints (IMPOSE), a physics-inspired framework that synthesizes identity-preserving, multi-pose contactless fingerprint samples to empower recognition models. IMPOSE consists of three stages: (1) rolled fingerprint identity generation via latent diffusion with discrete codebook representations, (2) cross-modal translation from rolled to contactless modality guided by Sauvola-based local adaptive binarization as an identity anchor, and (3) physics-based multi-pose simulation through 3D finger model texture mapping and projection. The generated samples maintain strict identity consistency at the ridge topology level and spatial alignment with standard fingerprint coordinate space. Extensive experiments on the UWA and PolyU CL2CB databases demonstrate that fine-tuning fixed-length dense descriptors (FDD) with IMPOSE-synthesized data achieves state-of-the-art cross-modal matching, reducing EER to 8.74% on UWA and 2.26% on PolyU CL2CB. Synthetic data also yields consistent gains across mainstream representations including DeepPrint and AFRNet, and the hybrid strategy combining synthetic and real data achieves the best overall results. The code and generated samples are available at https://github.com/Yu-Yy/IMPOSE.

ai.tldr: 提出IMPOSE框架，通过物理启发的多姿态生成合成身份一致的接触式指纹样本，提升跨模态匹配性能。
ai.motivation: 接触式指纹因无物理约束导致严重非线性几何畸变，现有方法在极端姿态下脆弱。
ai.method: 三阶段：潜扩散生成卷滚指纹、Sauvola二值化引导跨模态翻译、3D纹理映射模拟多姿态。
ai.result: 在UWA和PolyU CL2CB上EER分别降至8.74%和2.26%，超越现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供合成数据增强跨模态指纹识别系统的代码和样本。

idea_spark.idea: 利用3D模型纹理映射和投影模拟真实采集中的几何畸变，无需真实多姿态数据。
idea_spark.risk: 合成数据与真实数据分布差异可能导致过拟合或泛化不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将物理约束融入生成过程，而非仅依赖数据驱动，可提升样本的真实性和一致性。

zh.tldr: 提出IMPOSE框架，通过物理启发的多姿态生成合成身份一致的接触式指纹样本，提升跨模态匹配性能。
zh.motivation: 接触式指纹因无物理约束导致严重非线性几何畸变，现有方法在极端姿态下脆弱。
zh.method: 三阶段：潜扩散生成卷滚指纹、Sauvola二值化引导跨模态翻译、3D纹理映射模拟多姿态。
zh.result: 在UWA和PolyU CL2CB上EER分别降至8.74%和2.26%，超越现有方法。
zh.help_to_user: 提供合成数据增强跨模态指纹识别系统的代码和样本。

idea_spark.idea: 利用3D模型纹理映射和投影模拟真实采集中的几何畸变，无需真实多姿态数据。
idea_spark.risk: 合成数据与真实数据分布差异可能导致过拟合或泛化不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将物理约束融入生成过程，而非仅依赖数据驱动，可提升样本的真实性和一致性。

en.tldr: Proposes IMPOSE, a physics-inspired framework that synthesizes identity-consistent multi-pose contactless fingerprint samples to improve cross-modal matching.
en.motivation: Contactless fingerprints suffer from severe nonlinear geometric distortions due to free poses; existing methods are fragile under extreme variations.
en.method: Three stages: latent diffusion for rolled fingerprint generation, Sauvola-guided cross-modal translation, and physics-based multi-pose simulation via 3D texture mapping and projection.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art EER of 8.74% on UWA and 2.26% on PolyU CL2CB, outperforming prior methods.
en.help_to_user: Provides code and synthetic samples to enhance cross-modal fingerprint recognition systems.

idea_spark.idea: Use 3D model texture mapping and projection to simulate real geometric distortions without requiring real multi-pose data.
idea_spark.risk: Distribution mismatch between synthetic and real data may cause overfitting or poor generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Incorporating physical constraints into generation, rather than purely data-driven approaches, improves sample realism and consistency.

papers.title: Formal moduli and the splitting theory of supermanifolds
papers.summary: We develop a formal moduli theory for the splitting problem of complex supermanifolds. Starting from Green's obstruction tower, we construct a finite-step filtered dg Lie algebra which controls splittings by filtered Maurer-Cartan theory. We prove that the classical successive obstruction classes are recovered as the leading terms of adapted Maurer-Cartan representatives, and we transfer the theory to a minimal filtered $L_\infty$-model whose higher brackets give the intrinsic Kuranishi relations among the obstruction coordinates. We also prove that, in a precise filtered sense, the affine Atiyah class contains the entire Green obstruction tower: the Donagi-Witten component gives the primary obstruction, while the higher obstructions arise as successive projected defects and residual classes of the same Atiyah cocycle. We then pass to families, by constructing the fixed-retract formal moduli problem with prescribed split model and by identifying the formal neighbourhood of the split section with the fibrewise deformation theory of the split model; under standard finiteness, base-change, and descent hypotheses this yields relative tangent-obstruction and Kuranishi presentations. Finally, we work out explicit examples of non-split supergeometries, including cases with residual higher obstructions and a first nonlinear Kuranishi relation. These examples illustrate the interaction between Green obstructions, Atiyah classes, and higher $L_\infty$-operations.

ai.tldr: 本文为复超流形的分裂问题建立了形式模空间理论，通过滤过微分分次李代数控制分裂，并揭示了Atiyah类与Green障碍塔的深层联系。
ai.motivation: 复超流形的分裂问题长期缺乏系统的形式模空间理论，现有方法难以统一处理逐次障碍与高阶结构。
ai.method: 从Green障碍塔出发构造有限步滤过dg李代数，利用滤过Maurer-Cartan理论控制分裂；进一步转移到极小滤过L_∞模型，其高括号给出内在Kuranishi关系；并证明仿射Atiyah类包含整个Green障碍塔。
ai.result: 经典逐次障碍类被恢复为适应Maurer-Cartan代表的首项；在族情形下构造了固定收缩的形式模空间问题，并给出了相对切-障碍与Kuranishi表示；通过显式非分裂超几何例子展示了残余高障碍与非线性Kuranishi关系。
ai.help_to_user: 本文为超流形分裂问题提供了严格的代数框架，有助于理解障碍类与Atiyah类的相互作用，并可用于构造新的非分裂超几何例子。

idea_spark.idea: 利用Atiyah类的高阶分量构造新的不变量，用于区分具有相同低阶障碍的非分裂超流形。
idea_spark.risk: 滤过结构的选取可能依赖于具体几何数据，通用性需进一步验证；高障碍项的计算可能非常复杂。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将障碍理论中的逐次逼近与同调代数中的谱序列思想结合，可能发展出更系统的障碍分类工具。

en.tldr: This paper develops a formal moduli theory for the splitting problem of complex supermanifolds, using filtered dg Lie algebras and L_∞ models to control obstructions, and reveals that the affine Atiyah class contains the entire Green obstruction tower.
en.motivation: The splitting problem of complex supermanifolds lacked a systematic formal moduli theory, and existing methods could not uniformly handle successive obstructions and higher structures.
en.method: Starting from Green's obstruction tower, a finite-step filtered dg Lie algebra is constructed to control splittings via filtered Maurer-Cartan theory; the theory is transferred to a minimal filtered L_∞-model whose higher brackets encode intrinsic Kuranishi relations; it is also shown that the affine Atiyah class contains the entire Green obstruction tower.
en.result: Classical successive obstruction classes are recovered as leading terms of adapted Maurer-Cartan representatives; for families, a fixed-retract formal moduli problem is constructed, yielding relative tangent-obstruction and Kuranishi presentations; explicit non-split supergeometries illustrate residual higher obstructions and a first nonlinear Kuranishi relation.
en.help_to_user: This paper provides a rigorous algebraic framework for the splitting problem of supermanifolds, clarifying the interplay between obstruction classes and Atiyah classes, and enabling the construction of new non-split supergeometric examples.

idea_spark.idea: Use higher components of the Atiyah class to construct new invariants that distinguish non-split supermanifolds with identical low-order obstructions.
idea_spark.risk: The choice of filtration may depend on specific geometric data, requiring further verification of generality; computation of higher obstructions can be highly complex.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining the successive approximation in obstruction theory with spectral sequence ideas from homological algebra may lead to a more systematic classification tool for obstructions.

papers.title: Video Generation Models as World Models: Efficient Paradigms, Architectures and Algorithms
papers.summary: The rapid evolution of video generation has enabled models to simulate complex physical dynamics and long-horizon causalities, positioning them as potential world simulators. However, a critical gap still remains between the theoretical capacity for world simulation and the heavy computational costs of spatiotemporal modeling. To address this, we comprehensively and systematically review video generation frameworks and techniques that consider efficiency as a crucial requirement for practical world modeling. We introduce a novel taxonomy in three dimensions: efficient modeling paradigms, efficient network architectures, and efficient inference algorithms. We further show that bridging this efficiency gap directly empowers interactive applications such as autonomous driving, embodied AI, and game simulation. Finally, we identify emerging research frontiers in efficient video-based world modeling, arguing that efficiency is a fundamental prerequisite for evolving video generators into general-purpose, real-time, and robust world simulators.

ai.tldr: 综述了将视频生成模型作为世界模型时，在高效建模范式、网络架构和推理算法方面的进展，并指出效率是实现通用实时世界模拟器的关键。
ai.motivation: 视频生成模型具备模拟物理动态和长期因果的潜力，但高昂的计算成本阻碍了其作为世界模拟器的实际应用。
ai.method: 从三个维度（高效建模范式、高效网络架构、高效推理算法）系统分类和综述现有技术，并展示效率提升如何赋能自动驾驶、具身AI和游戏模拟等交互应用。
ai.result: 识别出高效视频世界建模的前沿研究方向，强调效率是视频生成器演变为通用实时鲁棒世界模拟器的基本前提。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究者快速了解视频生成模型作为世界模型的高效技术路线，包括范式、架构和算法，并指明未来方向。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将视频生成模型中的高效推理算法（如蒸馏、剪枝）应用于自动驾驶的在线场景预测。
idea_spark.risk: 过度追求效率可能牺牲模型对复杂物理细节的模拟精度，需在效率与保真度间权衡。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受“效率是通用世界模拟器前提”启发，可设计自适应计算分配机制，根据场景复杂度动态调整模型计算量。

en.tldr: A comprehensive survey on video generation models as world models, focusing on efficient paradigms, architectures, and algorithms, arguing that efficiency is key to real-time world simulation.
en.motivation: Video generation models can simulate physical dynamics and long-term causality but suffer from high computational costs, hindering their use as practical world simulators.
en.method: Proposes a three-dimensional taxonomy (efficient modeling paradigms, network architectures, inference algorithms) and reviews techniques that bridge the efficiency gap, enabling applications like autonomous driving, embodied AI, and game simulation.
en.result: Identifies emerging research frontiers in efficient video-based world modeling, emphasizing efficiency as a prerequisite for evolving video generators into general-purpose, real-time, robust world simulators.
en.help_to_user: Provides a structured overview of efficient techniques for using video generation as world models, helping researchers identify key approaches and future directions.

idea_spark.idea: Apply efficient inference algorithms (e.g., distillation, pruning) from video generation to online scene prediction in autonomous driving.
idea_spark.risk: Overemphasis on efficiency may compromise the fidelity of complex physical details; a trade-off between efficiency and accuracy is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the claim that efficiency is a prerequisite for universal world simulators, design adaptive computation allocation mechanisms that adjust model compute based on scene complexity.

papers.title: Effective Performance Measurement: Challenges and Opportunities in KPI Extraction from Earnings Calls
papers.summary: Earnings calls are a key source of financial information about public companies. However, extracting information from these calls is difficult. Unlike the templatic filings required by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to report a company's financial situation, earnings conference calls have no built-in labels, are unstructured, and feature conversational language. We explore this challenging domain by assessing the information captured by models trained on SEC filings and in-context learning methods. To establish a baseline, we first evaluate the generalization capabilities of SEC-trained models across established SEC datasets. To support our investigation, we introduce three novel benchmarks: (1) SEC Filings Benchmark (SECB), (2) Earnings Calls Benchmark (ECB), and ECB-A, a subset with 2,460 expert annotation groups to support our qualitative analysis. We find that encoder-based models struggle with the domain shift. Finally, we propose a system utilizing LLMs to perform open-ended extraction from unstructured call transcripts, verified by human evaluation (79.7% precision), providing a baseline for this valuable domain through the consistent tracking of emergent KPIs.

ai.tldr: 本文研究从非结构化的财报电话会议中提取关键绩效指标（KPI）的挑战，提出基于大语言模型的系统，并通过人工评估验证其有效性。
ai.motivation: 财报电话会议是重要的财务信息来源，但缺乏标签、结构松散且语言口语化，现有基于SEC模板文件的模型难以直接泛化。
ai.method: 构建三个新基准数据集（SECB、ECB、ECB-A），评估编码器模型在领域迁移下的表现，并设计基于大语言模型的开放式KPI提取系统。
ai.result: 编码器模型在领域迁移中表现不佳；所提系统在人工评估中达到79.7%的精确率，为新兴KPI的持续追踪提供了基线。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何从非结构化财务文本中自动提取关键绩效指标，并了解当前方法的局限与改进方向。

idea_spark.idea: 结合领域微调与提示工程，进一步提升大语言模型在特定财务术语上的提取准确性。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖人工标注质量，且大语言模型可能产生幻觉，需设计验证机制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将SEC模板文件的标签知识作为弱监督信号，辅助电话会议文本的标注与模型训练。

idea_spark.risk: 依赖人工标注质量，且大语言模型可能产生幻觉，需设计验证机制。

en.tldr: This paper studies the challenge of extracting key performance indicators (KPIs) from unstructured earnings calls, proposes an LLM-based system, and validates its effectiveness through human evaluation.
en.motivation: Earnings calls are a vital source of financial information but lack labels, structure, and contain conversational language, making existing SEC-filing-trained models hard to generalize.
en.method: Construct three novel benchmarks (SECB, ECB, ECB-A), evaluate encoder models under domain shift, and design an LLM-based open-ended KPI extraction system.
en.result: Encoder models struggle with domain shift; the proposed system achieves 79.7% precision in human evaluation, providing a baseline for tracking emergent KPIs.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to automatically extract KPIs from unstructured financial text and highlights current limitations and improvement directions.

idea_spark.idea: Combine domain fine-tuning with prompt engineering to further improve LLM extraction accuracy for specific financial terms.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on annotation quality; LLMs may hallucinate, requiring verification mechanisms.
idea_spark.inspiration: Use label knowledge from SEC filings as weak supervision to aid annotation and model training for earnings call transcripts.

papers.title: CuraView: A Multi-Agent Framework for Medical Hallucination Detection with GraphRAG-Enhanced Knowledge Verification
papers.summary: Discharge summaries require extracting critical information from lengthy electronic health records (EHRs), a process that is labor-intensive when performed manually. Large language models (LLMs) can improve generation efficiency; however, they are prone to producing faithfulness hallucinations, statements that contradict source records, posing direct risks to patient safety. To address this, we present CuraView, a multi-agent framework for sentence-level detection and evidence-grounded explanation of faithfulness hallucinations in discharge summaries. CuraView constructs a GraphRAG-based knowledge graph from patient-level EHRs and implements a closed-loop generation-detection pipeline with sentence-level evidence retrieval and classification spanning four evidence grades from strong support to direct contradiction (E1-E4), yielding structured and interpretable evidence chains.   We evaluate CuraView on a subset of 250 patients from the Discharge-Me benchmark, with 50 patients held out for testing. Our fine-tuned Qwen3-14B detection model achieves an F1 of 0.831 on the safety-critical E4 metric (90.9% recall, 76.5% precision) and an F1 of 0.823 on E3+E4, representing a 50.0% relative improvement over the base model and outperforming RAGTruth-style and QAGS-style baselines. These results demonstrate that evidence-chain-based graph retrieval verification substantially improves the factual reliability of clinical documentation, while simultaneously producing reusable annotated datasets for downstream model training and distillation.

ai.tldr: 提出CuraView多智能体框架，利用GraphRAG知识图谱和证据链实现出院小结忠实性幻觉的句子级检测与可解释验证。
ai.motivation: 大语言模型生成的出院小结常出现与原始病历矛盾的忠实性幻觉，直接威胁患者安全，现有方法缺乏细粒度证据支撑。
ai.method: 构建基于GraphRAG的患者级知识图谱，设计闭环生成-检测流水线，包含句子级证据检索和四级证据等级（E1-E4）分类，生成结构化可解释证据链。
ai.result: 在Discharge-Me基准的50例测试集上，微调Qwen3-14B模型在安全关键指标E4上F1达0.831（召回率90.9%，精确率76.5%），E3+E4的F1为0.823，相对基础模型提升50.0%，优于RAGTruth和QAGS基线。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助临床文档审核人员快速定位并理解LLM生成内容中的事实错误，提升出院小结的可靠性。

idea_spark.idea: 将GraphRAG与多智能体协作结合，通过闭环流水线实现幻觉的自动检测与证据追溯，同时产出可复用的标注数据集。
idea_spark.risk: 知识图谱构建依赖高质量结构化EHR数据，数据稀疏或噪声可能影响检索效果；四级证据分类的边界定义需专家校准。
idea_spark.inspiration: 证据等级（E1-E4）的设计为幻觉检测提供了可量化的可信度标尺，启发在其他生成任务中引入类似的分级验证机制。

en.tldr: Proposes CuraView, a multi-agent framework for sentence-level faithfulness hallucination detection in discharge summaries using GraphRAG knowledge graphs and evidence chains.
en.motivation: LLM-generated discharge summaries often contain faithfulness hallucinations contradicting source records, posing patient safety risks; existing methods lack fine-grained evidence.
en.method: Constructs a patient-level GraphRAG knowledge graph and implements a closed-loop generation-detection pipeline with sentence-level evidence retrieval and four-grade classification (E1-E4) for interpretable evidence chains.
en.result: On 50 test patients from Discharge-Me, fine-tuned Qwen3-14B achieves F1 0.831 on safety-critical E4 (90.9% recall, 76.5% precision) and F1 0.823 on E3+E4, a 50.0% relative improvement over base model, outperforming RAGTruth and QAGS baselines.
en.help_to_user: Helps clinicians quickly identify and understand factual errors in LLM-generated discharge summaries, improving documentation reliability.

idea_spark.idea: Combines GraphRAG with multi-agent collaboration in a closed-loop pipeline for automatic hallucination detection and evidence tracing, while producing reusable annotated datasets.
idea_spark.risk: Knowledge graph construction relies on high-quality structured EHR data; data sparsity or noise may degrade retrieval; grade boundaries require expert calibration.
idea_spark.inspiration: The evidence grade design (E1-E4) provides a quantifiable credibility scale for hallucination detection, inspiring similar graded verification mechanisms in other generation tasks.

papers.title: Higher categories of bordisms with geometric structures
papers.summary: We introduce a system of axioms that uniquely defines an (infinity,d)-category of bordisms equipped with geometric data. The underlying manifolds of these bordisms may be smooth, complex, super, or formal smooth manifolds, as well as any class of manifolds satisfying conditions specified in this paper. We develop a general notion of a field on a manifold, encompassing structures such as Riemannian metrics, principal bundles with connection, conformal structures, and traditional tangential structures. Using this framework, we construct a symmetric monoidal (infinity,d)-category of bordisms with prescribed underlying manifolds and fields, and prove that it satisfies our axioms.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一个公理系统，唯一地定义了带有几何数据的 (∞,d)-范畴的配边。
ai.motivation: 为带有各种几何结构的配边建立统一的范畴论框架。
ai.method: 通过定义流形上的场（包括黎曼度量、主丛联络、共形结构等），构造对称幺半 (∞,d)-范畴并证明其满足公理。
ai.result: 成功构造了满足公理的对称幺半 (∞,d)-范畴，适用于光滑、复、超、形式光滑等多种流形。
ai.help_to_user: 为研究几何配边范畴提供了严格的公理化基础。

en.tldr: This paper introduces an axiomatic system that uniquely defines an (∞,d)-category of bordisms with geometric data.
en.motivation: To establish a unified categorical framework for bordisms equipped with various geometric structures.
en.method: By defining fields on manifolds (including Riemannian metrics, principal bundles with connection, conformal structures, etc.), a symmetric monoidal (∞,d)-category is constructed and shown to satisfy the axioms.
en.result: A symmetric monoidal (∞,d)-category satisfying the axioms is successfully constructed, applicable to smooth, complex, super, formal smooth, and other manifolds.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous axiomatic foundation for the study of geometric bordism categories.

idea_spark.idea: Abstract the notion of fields to unify multiple geometric structures.
idea_spark.risk: The generality of the axioms may limit applicability to certain special structures.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires applying similar frameworks to bordism classification in quantum field theory or string theory.

papers.title: A Robust Unsupervised Domain Adaptation Framework for Medical Image Classification Using RKHS-MMD
papers.summary: Labeling medical images is a major bottleneck in the field of medical imaging, as it requires domain-specific expertise, and it gets further complicated due to variability across different medical centers and different imaging devices. Such heterogeneity introduces domain shifts and modality discrepancies, which limits the generalization of trained models. To address this important challenge, we propose an unsupervised domain adaptation framework that combines transfer learning with a Reproducing Kernel Hilbert Space based Maximum Mean Discrepancy loss for the alignment of source and target domains. By jointly optimizing classification and RKHS-MMD losses, the methodology enhances generalization to unannotated medical datasets while diminishing reliance on manual annotation. Experimental evaluations presented on two chest X-ray datasets, which are obtained from different medical centers, show outstanding improvements over models trained without adaptation. Furthermore, we perform a comparative study to see that RKHS-MMD performs better than the standard Maximum Mean Discrepancy in reducing modality gap, emphasizing its effectiveness for medical image classification and also its strong capability in advanced AI-driven medical diagnostics.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于RKHS-MMD的无监督域适应框架，用于医学图像分类，在胸部X光数据集上显著提升跨域泛化能力。
ai.motivation: 医学图像标注成本高，且不同医疗中心和设备导致域偏移，限制模型泛化。
ai.method: 结合迁移学习与再生核希尔伯特空间最大均值差异损失，联合优化分类与域对齐。
ai.result: 在两个胸部X光数据集上，相比未适应模型表现优异，且RKHS-MMD优于标准MMD。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户无需标注即可将模型应用于新医疗中心的未标注数据。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将RKHS-MMD与自监督学习结合，进一步提升无监督域适应性能。
idea_spark.risk: RKHS核函数选择可能影响效果，需针对不同数据分布调优。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用核方法度量分布差异，启发设计更鲁棒的域适应损失函数。

en.tldr: Proposes an unsupervised domain adaptation framework using RKHS-MMD for medical image classification, significantly improving cross-domain generalization on chest X-ray datasets.
en.motivation: Labeling medical images is costly and domain shifts across centers and devices limit model generalization.
en.method: Combines transfer learning with Reproducing Kernel Hilbert Space Maximum Mean Discrepancy loss to jointly optimize classification and domain alignment.
en.result: Outperforms non-adapted models on two chest X-ray datasets, with RKHS-MMD surpassing standard MMD.
en.help_to_user: Enables users to apply models to unlabeled data from new medical centers without manual annotation.

idea_spark.risk: Choice of kernel function may affect performance and requires tuning for different data distributions.

papers.title: NucEval: A Robust Evaluation Framework for Nuclear Instance Segmentation
papers.summary: In computational pathology, nuclear instance segmentation is a fundamental task with many downstream clinical applications. With the advent of deep learning, many approaches, including convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and vision transformers (ViTs), have been proposed for this task, along with both machine learning-based and non-machine learning-based pre- and post-processing techniques to further boost performance. However, one fundamental aspect that has received less attention is the evaluation pipeline. In this study, we identify four key issues associated with nuclear instance segmentation evaluation and propose corresponding solutions. Our proposed modifications, namely handling vague regions, score normalization, overlapping instances, and border uncertainty, are integrated into a unified framework called NucEval, which enables robust evaluation of nuclear instance segmentation. We evaluate this pipeline using the NuInsSeg dataset, which provides unique characteristics that make it particularly suitable for this study, as well as two additional external datasets, with three CNN- and ViT-based nuclear instance segmentation models, to demonstrate the impact of these modifications on instance segmentation metrics. The code, along with complete guidelines and illustrative examples, is publicly available at: https://github.com/masih4/nuc_eval.

ai.tldr: 提出NucEval评估框架，解决核实例分割中的四个关键问题：模糊区域、分数归一化、重叠实例和边界不确定性。
ai.motivation: 现有核实例分割评估流程存在不足，影响下游临床应用的可靠性。
ai.method: 通过处理模糊区域、分数归一化、重叠实例和边界不确定性，集成到统一框架NucEval中。
ai.result: 在NuInsSeg等数据集上验证，展示了改进对分割指标的影响。
ai.help_to_user: 提供更稳健的核实例分割评估方法，代码和指南公开可用。

idea_spark.idea: 将模糊区域和边界不确定性处理模块化，适配不同分割模型。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖特定数据集特性，泛化性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从评估角度提升分割模型可靠性，而非仅改进模型架构。
