en.method: Integrates handling of vague regions, score normalization, overlapping instances, and border uncertainty into a unified framework NucEval.
en.result: Validated on NuInsSeg and other datasets, demonstrating impact on segmentation metrics.
en.help_to_user: Provides a more robust evaluation method for nuclear instance segmentation, with publicly available code and guidelines.

idea_spark.idea: Modularize handling of vague regions and border uncertainty to adapt to different segmentation models.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on specific dataset characteristics; generalizability needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Improving segmentation model reliability from the evaluation perspective, not just model architecture.

papers.title: SPEC CPU2026: Characterization, Representativeness, and Cross-Suite Comparison
papers.summary: Specialized accelerators dominate AI workloads, but CPUs remain critical for orchestrating these accelerators and running datacenter services. As a result, CPU performance increasingly shapes end-to-end system efficiency, making it necessary for benchmarks to reflect modern workloads and bottlenecks. However, it remains unclear how emerging CPU benchmark suites reflect these shifts. To address this, we present the first comprehensive characterization of SPEC CPU2026 across nine platforms spanning recent Intel, AMD, Ampere, and Nvidia processors. We find that, compared to SPEC CPU2017, SPEC CPU2026 increases instruction volume and memory footprint, and shifts pressure toward emerging bottlenecks, most notably higher instruction-cache stress. We next examine whether the full suite is necessary for architectural evaluation. Using clustering-based representativeness analysis, we identify that compact subsets of 4-5 workloads per group preserve 96.4-99.9% of full-suite behavior, substantially reducing evaluation costs without sacrificing fidelity. To better position SPEC CPU2026, we compare it against SPEC CPU2017, DCPerf, and MLPerf using cross-suite microarchitectural metrics. SPEC CPU2026 remains a general-purpose suite with complementary characteristics: it is less vector-intensive than MLPerf and has lower frontend pressure than DCPerf, yet moves closer to real-world CPU behavior than prior SPEC CPU generations. Finally, we show that SPEC CPU2026 supports practical architectural studies beyond aggregate scores through case studies on page sizes and allocators, prefetching, compiler optimizations, ISA sensitivity, and many-core scaling. The new round-robin stagger mode generates proxy workloads that approximate DCPerf, reducing the IPC gap to 13.7%. Overall, SPEC CPU2026 sets a new foundation for rigorous and cost-effective CPU evaluation.

ai.tldr: 首次全面分析SPEC CPU2026基准测试套件，发现其相比SPEC CPU2017增加了指令量和内存占用，并转向指令缓存压力等新瓶颈；通过聚类分析，4-5个工作负载的子集可保留96.4-99.9%的全套行为；跨套件比较显示其通用性，并支持多种实际架构研究。
ai.motivation: CPU在AI加速器协调和数据中心服务中仍至关重要，但现有基准测试能否反映现代工作负载和瓶颈尚不清楚，因此需要系统评估SPEC CPU2026。
ai.method: 在九种Intel、AMD、Ampere和Nvidia平台上进行微架构特征分析，使用聚类方法进行代表性分析，并与SPEC CPU2017、DCPerf和MLPerf进行跨套件比较。
ai.result: SPEC CPU2026指令量和内存占用增加，指令缓存压力显著；紧凑子集（每组4-5个负载）保持高保真度；跨套件比较显示其通用性；轮询交错模式可生成逼近DCPerf的代理负载，IPC差距降至13.7%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解SPEC CPU2026的特性、代表性及与旧套件的差异，指导选择精简子集以降低评估成本，并提供实际架构研究案例。

idea_spark.idea: 基于SPEC CPU2026的代理负载生成方法可扩展至其他领域，如为特定硬件设计定制化微基准。
idea_spark.risk: 紧凑子集可能遗漏某些极端或罕见瓶颈，需验证其在不同架构上的泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 轮询交错模式启发了一种低成本生成代表性代理负载的思路，可用于快速原型评估。

en.tldr: First comprehensive characterization of SPEC CPU2026 across nine platforms, showing increased instruction volume and memory footprint with shift toward instruction-cache stress; clustering identifies compact subsets preserving 96.4-99.9% of full-suite behavior; cross-suite comparison highlights its general-purpose nature; round-robin stagger mode generates proxy workloads reducing IPC gap to 13.7%.
en.motivation: CPUs remain critical for orchestrating accelerators and running datacenter services, but it is unclear whether emerging CPU benchmarks reflect modern workloads and bottlenecks, necessitating a systematic evaluation of SPEC CPU2026.
en.method: Microarchitectural characterization on nine Intel, AMD, Ampere, and Nvidia platforms; clustering-based representativeness analysis; cross-suite comparison with SPEC CPU2017, DCPerf, and MLPerf.
en.result: SPEC CPU2026 increases instruction volume and memory footprint, with higher instruction-cache stress; compact subsets (4-5 workloads per group) preserve high fidelity; cross-suite comparison shows general-purpose nature; round-robin stagger mode generates proxy workloads approximating DCPerf with IPC gap reduced to 13.7%.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand SPEC CPU2026 characteristics, representativeness, and differences from prior suites, guides selection of compact subsets to reduce evaluation costs, and provides practical architectural study cases.

idea_spark.idea: The proxy workload generation approach from SPEC CPU2026 can be extended to other domains, such as designing custom microbenchmarks for specific hardware.
idea_spark.risk: Compact subsets may miss extreme or rare bottlenecks; generalizability across different architectures needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: The round-robin stagger mode inspires a low-cost approach to generate representative proxy workloads for rapid prototyping evaluation.

papers.title: The Detector Teaches Itself: Lightweight Self-Supervised Adaptation for Open-Vocabulary Object Detection
papers.summary: Open-vocabulary object detection aims to recognize objects from an open set of categories, which leverages vision-language models (VLMs) pre-trained on large-scale image-text data. The cooperative paradigm combines an object detector with a VLM to achieve zero-shot recognition of novel objects. However, VLMs pre-trained on full images often struggle to capture local object details, limiting their effectiveness when applied to region-level detection. We present Decoupled Adaptivity Training (DAT), a self-supervised fine-tuning approach to improve VLMs for cooperative model-based object detection. Given a cooperative model consists of a closed-set detector and a VLM, we first construct a region-aware pseudo-labeled dataset using a pre-trained closed-set object detector, in which regions corresponding to novel objects may be present but remain unlabeled or mislabeled. We then fine-tune the visual backbone of the VLM in a decoupled manner, which enhances local feature alignment while preserving global semantic knowledge via weight interpolation. DAT is a plug-and-play module that requires no inference overhead and fine-tunes less than 0.8M parameters. Experiments on the COCO and LVIS datasets show that DAT consistently improves detection performance on both novel and known categories, establishing a new state of the art in cooperative open-vocabulary detection.

ai.tldr: 提出一种轻量级自监督微调方法DAT，通过解耦训练和权重插值提升视觉语言模型在开放词汇目标检测中的区域特征对齐能力，无需额外推理开销。
ai.motivation: 视觉语言模型在全图预训练后难以捕捉局部目标细节，限制了其在区域级开放词汇检测中的效果。
ai.method: 利用预训练闭集检测器构建区域感知伪标签数据集，对VLM视觉骨干进行解耦微调，并通过权重插值保持全局语义知识。
ai.result: 在COCO和LVIS数据集上，DAT在已知和未知类别上均提升检测性能，达到开放词汇检测新标杆。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种即插即用的自监督微调方案，可增强现有开放词汇检测模型对局部细节的识别能力。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将DAT与多模态大模型结合，利用更丰富的语言先验进一步改善开放词汇检测的零样本泛化。
idea_spark.risk: 伪标签质量依赖闭集检测器，若检测器对未知类别产生大量错误标签可能引入噪声。
idea_spark.inspiration: 权重插值平衡局部与全局特征的思想可启发设计更通用的视觉语言模型微调框架。

zh.tldr: 提出一种轻量级自监督微调方法DAT，通过解耦训练和权重插值提升视觉语言模型在开放词汇目标检测中的区域特征对齐能力，无需额外推理开销。
zh.motivation: 视觉语言模型在全图预训练后难以捕捉局部目标细节，限制了其在区域级开放词汇检测中的效果。

zh.help_to_user: 提供一种即插即用的自监督微调方案，可增强现有开放词汇检测模型对局部细节的识别能力。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将DAT与多模态大模型结合，利用更丰富的语言先验进一步改善开放词汇检测的零样本泛化。
idea_spark.risk: 伪标签质量依赖闭集检测器，若检测器对未知类别产生大量错误标签可能引入噪声。
idea_spark.inspiration: 权重插值平衡局部与全局特征的思想可启发设计更通用的视觉语言模型微调框架。

en.tldr: Proposes DAT, a lightweight self-supervised fine-tuning method that improves region-level feature alignment of VLMs for open-vocabulary detection via decoupled training and weight interpolation, with no inference overhead.
en.motivation: VLMs pre-trained on full images struggle to capture local object details, limiting their effectiveness in region-level open-vocabulary detection.
en.method: Constructs region-aware pseudo-labeled dataset using a pre-trained closed-set detector, then fine-tunes the VLM visual backbone in a decoupled manner with weight interpolation to preserve global semantics.
en.result: Consistently improves detection on both novel and known categories on COCO and LVIS, achieving state-of-the-art in cooperative open-vocabulary detection.
en.help_to_user: Provides a plug-and-play self-supervised fine-tuning approach to enhance region-level recognition in existing open-vocabulary detection models.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining DAT with multimodal large models to leverage richer language priors for further improving zero-shot generalization in open-vocabulary detection.
idea_spark.risk: Pseudo-label quality depends on the closed-set detector; incorrect labels for novel categories may introduce noise.
idea_spark.inspiration: The idea of balancing local and global features via weight interpolation can inspire more general fine-tuning frameworks for vision-language models.

papers.title: Correct Is Not Enough: Training Reasoning Planners with Executor-Grounded Rewards
papers.summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards has become a common way to improve explicit reasoning in large language models, but final-answer correctness alone does not reveal whether the reasoning trace is faithful, reliable, or useful to the model that consumes it. This outcome-only signal can reinforce traces that are right for the wrong reasons, overstate reasoning gains by rewarding shortcuts, and propagate flawed intermediate states in multi-step systems. To this end, we propose TraceLift, a planner-executor training framework that treats reasoning as a consumable intermediate artifact. During planner training, the planner emits tagged reasoning. A frozen executor turns this reasoning into the final artifact for verifier feedback, while an executor-grounded reward shapes the intermediate trace. This reward multiplies a rubric-based Reasoning Reward Model (RM) score by measured uplift on the same frozen executor, crediting traces that are both high-quality and useful. To make reasoning quality directly learnable, we introduce TRACELIFT-GROUPS, a rubric-annotated reason-only dataset built from math and code seed problems. Each example is a same-problem group containing a high-quality reference trace and multiple plausible flawed traces with localized perturbations that reduce reasoning quality or solution support while preserving task relevance. Extensive experiments on code and math benchmarks show that this executor-grounded reasoning reward improves the two-stage planner-executor system over execution-only training, suggesting that reasoning supervision should evaluate not only whether a trace looks good, but also whether it helps the model that consumes it.

ai.tldr: 提出TraceLift框架，通过执行器接地奖励训练推理规划器，使推理轨迹不仅正确而且对下游执行器有用。
ai.motivation: 仅依赖最终答案正确性训练推理模型会强化虚假推理、夸大推理增益，且在多步系统中传播错误中间状态。
ai.method: 冻结执行器，规划器输出带标签推理，执行器将其转化为最终结果；奖励为基于规则的推理奖励模型分数乘以执行器性能提升，并构建TRACELIFT-GROUPS数据集。
ai.result: 在代码和数学基准上，执行器接地推理奖励优于仅执行训练，表明推理监督应评估轨迹是否对消费模型有帮助。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户训练更可靠、对下游任务更有用的推理模型，减少虚假推理。

idea_spark.idea: 将推理视为可消费中间产物，用执行器反馈优化推理质量。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖冻结执行器，可能限制规划器探索；奖励设计复杂。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从“正确但无用”的推理问题出发，引入执行器接地奖励，启发更细粒度的推理监督。

en.tldr: Proposes TraceLift, a framework that trains reasoning planners with executor-grounded rewards, ensuring reasoning traces are not only correct but also useful for downstream executors.
en.motivation: Training reasoning models solely on final-answer correctness can reinforce spurious reasoning, overstate gains, and propagate flawed intermediate states in multi-step systems.
en.method: A frozen executor converts planner's tagged reasoning into final output; reward multiplies rubric-based Reasoning Reward Model score by executor performance uplift; constructs TRACELIFT-GROUPS dataset.
en.result: On code and math benchmarks, executor-grounded reasoning reward outperforms execution-only training, showing reasoning supervision should evaluate whether traces help the consuming model.
en.help_to_user: Helps users train more reliable and downstream-task-useful reasoning models, reducing spurious reasoning.

idea_spark.idea: Treat reasoning as a consumable intermediate artifact and use executor feedback to optimize reasoning quality.
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on frozen executor may limit planner exploration; reward design is complex.
idea_spark.inspiration: Starting from the problem of 'correct but useless' reasoning, introduces executor-grounded rewards, inspiring finer-grained reasoning supervision.

papers.title: Anisotropy in Fourier space optical memory effect correlations
papers.summary: We investigate anisotropy in Fourier-domain speckle correlations associated with the optical memory effect in disordered scattering media. Within a single scattering framework, we show that while the conventional memory effect constrains transverse wavevector shifts, the correlation strength also depends non-trivially on differences in the axial wavevector components. Our theory is supported by numerical simulations of a three-dimensional, single scattering medium, which show excellent agreement with theory. We extend the analysis to pseudo-correlations, demonstrating that analogous anisotropic behavior arises in the conjugate memory effect. Our results highlight the often neglected role of axial disorder in scattered field correlations.

ai.tldr: 研究了无序散射介质中傅里叶域散斑相关性的各向异性，发现轴向波矢分量对相关强度有非平凡影响。
ai.motivation: 传统光学记忆效应仅考虑横向波矢偏移，忽略了轴向无序对散射场相关性的作用。
ai.method: 在单散射框架下理论推导，并通过三维单散射介质的数值模拟验证。
ai.result: 相关强度不仅受横向波矢偏移约束，还依赖于轴向波矢分量差异；类似各向异性行为在共轭记忆效应中同样出现。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解散射介质中光场相关性的完整维度，为成像和传感应用提供理论指导。

idea_spark.idea: 利用轴向波矢相关性设计新型散斑成像方法，提升深度分辨能力。
idea_spark.risk: 轴向效应在强散射或厚介质中可能被淹没，实验验证需精细控制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将轴向无序纳入散射相关模型，改进光学相干断层扫描等技术。

en.tldr: We investigate anisotropy in Fourier-domain speckle correlations related to the optical memory effect, showing that axial wavevector components non-trivially affect correlation strength.
en.motivation: Conventional optical memory effect only considers transverse wavevector shifts, neglecting the role of axial disorder in scattered field correlations.
en.method: Theoretical analysis within a single scattering framework, supported by numerical simulations of a 3D single scattering medium.
en.result: Correlation strength depends on both transverse and axial wavevector differences; analogous anisotropic behavior appears in the conjugate memory effect.
en.help_to_user: Provides a complete understanding of light field correlations in scattering media, guiding imaging and sensing applications.

idea_spark.idea: Exploit axial wavevector correlations to design novel speckle imaging methods with enhanced depth resolution.
idea_spark.risk: Axial effects may be masked in strongly scattering or thick media, requiring careful experimental control.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires incorporating axial disorder into scattering correlation models to improve techniques like optical coherence tomography.

papers.title: Using Large Language Models as a Co-Author in Undergraduate Quantum Group Research
papers.summary: This article describes the use of Claude CLI and its Opus 4.6 model, as a tool for writing an entirely AI-generated mathematics research paper. The resulting paper is comparable in scope and quality to papers previously produced by advanced undergraduate students in eight-week summer REU programs advised by the author. The main result is a new explicit formula for a central element of $U_q(\mathfrak{so}_{12})$, which can be used for an interacting particle system with Markov duality. Using SageMath and a sparse PBW-basis pairing matrix that admits symbolic inversion, Claude reduced the central-element computation by several orders of magnitude: a calculation that took 60 hours in a 2023 Python implementation completed in under a minute on a laptop. The article reflects on the implications for undergraduate research mentorship: if generative AI can now produce research of REU caliber, advisors must select problems that better demonstrate the qualities valued by graduate admissions committees. Limitations including poor runtime estimates and literal handling of differing mathematical conventions are documented.

ai.tldr: 本文展示了使用Claude CLI和Opus 4.6模型作为合著者，完全由AI生成一篇量子群数学研究论文，其质量与本科生REU项目相当，并提出了U_q(so_12)中心元的新显式公式。
ai.motivation: 探索大语言模型在本科生量子群研究中的合著能力，评估其能否产出达到REU水平的数学研究论文。
ai.method: 使用Claude CLI和Opus 4.6模型，结合SageMath和稀疏PBW基配对矩阵的符号求逆，大幅加速中心元计算。
ai.result: 生成了与本科生REU论文相当的研究论文，提出了U_q(so_12)中心元的新显式公式，计算时间从60小时降至1分钟以内。
ai.help_to_user: 展示了如何利用大语言模型加速量子群中的符号计算，并提供了本科生研究指导的新视角。

idea_spark.idea: 利用大语言模型自动生成并优化PBW基配对矩阵的符号求逆流程，从而高效计算中心元。
idea_spark.risk: 大语言模型可能误解数学约定或产生不可靠的运行时估计，需人工验证结果。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将大语言模型作为本科生研究中的协作工具，重新设计研究问题以突出人类价值。

en.tldr: This paper demonstrates using Claude CLI and Opus 4.6 as a co-author to produce an entirely AI-generated quantum group mathematics paper of REU quality, with a new explicit formula for a central element of U_q(so_12).
en.motivation: To explore the capability of large language models as co-authors in undergraduate quantum group research and assess whether they can produce research of REU caliber.
en.method: Using Claude CLI and Opus 4.6, combined with SageMath and symbolic inversion of a sparse PBW-basis pairing matrix to dramatically accelerate central-element computation.
en.result: Produced a research paper comparable to undergraduate REU output, with a new explicit formula for a central element of U_q(so_12), reducing computation time from 60 hours to under a minute.
en.help_to_user: Demonstrates how to leverage large language models to accelerate symbolic computation in quantum groups and provides new insights for undergraduate research mentorship.

idea_spark.idea: Use LLMs to automatically generate and optimize symbolic inversion of PBW-basis pairing matrices for efficient central-element computation.
idea_spark.risk: LLMs may misinterpret mathematical conventions or produce unreliable runtime estimates, requiring human verification of results.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires using LLMs as collaborative tools in undergraduate research, redesigning problems to highlight human-added value.

papers.title: A Comprehensive Analysis of Tokenization and Self-Supervised Learning in End-to-End Automatic Speech Recognition applied on French Language
papers.summary: The performance of end-to-end automatic speech recognition (ASR) systems enables their increasing integration into numerous applications. While there are various benefits to such speech-to-text systems, the choice of hyperparameters and models plays a crucial role in their performance. Typically, these choices are determined by considering only the character (CER) and/or word error rate (WER) metrics. However, it has been shown in several studies that these metrics are largely incomplete and fail to adequately describe the downstream application of automatic transcripts. In this paper, we conduct a qualitative study on the French language that investigates the impact of subword tokenization algorithms and self-supervised learning models from different linguistic and acoustic perspectives, using a comprehensive set of evaluation metrics.

ai.tldr: 本文针对法语端到端自动语音识别系统，全面分析了子词分词算法和自监督学习模型对识别性能的影响，并采用多种评估指标进行定性研究。
ai.motivation: 现有研究在选择超参数和模型时仅依赖字符错误率和词错误率，但这些指标不足以全面描述转录文本的下游应用效果。
ai.method: 在法语上开展定性研究，从语言和声学角度考察不同子词分词算法和自监督学习模型，使用综合评估指标集。
ai.result: 揭示了不同分词和自监督方法在多种评估维度上的表现差异，表明仅靠CER/WER无法全面反映系统性能。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员和工程师更全面地评估和选择法语ASR系统的分词策略与预训练模型。

idea_spark.idea: 构建一个多维度自动评估工具，集成语言学、声学及下游任务指标，辅助ASR模型选型。
idea_spark.risk: 评估指标增多可能导致调参复杂度上升，且部分指标可能相互冲突。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴自然语言处理中多任务评估的思路，将ASR评估从单一错误率扩展到语义、流畅性等维度。

idea_spark.idea: 构建一个多维度自动评估工具，集成语言学、声学及下游任务指标，辅助ASR模型选型。
idea_spark.risk: 评估指标增多可能导致调参复杂度上升，且部分指标可能相互冲突。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴自然语言处理中多任务评估的思路，将ASR评估从单一错误率扩展到语义、流畅性等维度。

en.tldr: This paper presents a qualitative study on French end-to-end ASR, analyzing the impact of subword tokenization and self-supervised learning models using a comprehensive set of evaluation metrics.
en.motivation: Existing hyperparameter and model choices rely solely on CER and WER, which are insufficient for describing downstream application quality.
en.method: A qualitative study on French, examining different subword tokenization algorithms and self-supervised learning models from linguistic and acoustic perspectives with a comprehensive metric set.
en.result: Reveals performance differences across multiple evaluation dimensions, showing that CER/WER alone cannot fully capture system effectiveness.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers and practitioners more comprehensively evaluate and select tokenization strategies and pretrained models for French ASR.

idea_spark.idea: Develop a multi-dimensional automatic evaluation tool integrating linguistic, acoustic, and downstream task metrics to guide ASR model selection.
idea_spark.risk: Increased number of metrics may raise tuning complexity and some metrics might conflict.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by multi-task evaluation in NLP, extending ASR evaluation from single error rate to semantic and fluency dimensions.

papers.title: Conditions for well-posed color recovery in scattering media
papers.summary: Recovering scene color from images captured in scattering media is a fundamental inverse problem in optical imaging. Yet the problem is intrinsically ill-posed as multiple solutions can explain the same observation, and prediction error cannot be controlled without understanding the space of candidate solutions. Here, we present sufficient conditions under which color recovery in a scattering medium becomes well-posed. Observing that ill-posedness stems from (i) projection of spectral signals onto pixel intensities, and (ii) unknown medium parameters, we demonstrate that sensor improvements alone cannot resolve medium-induced distortions without additional constraints. We identify recovery patterns, cross-pixel relationships that naturally occur in images, and prove, for an ideal hyperspectral camera, that they restrict the solution to a unique candidate. This opens the door to a new class of vision algorithms grounded in first principles, enabling quantitative analysis of images in scattering environments.

ai.tldr: 提出了在散射介质中颜色恢复成为适定问题的充分条件，并证明理想高光谱相机下交叉像素关系可唯一确定解。
ai.motivation: 散射介质中的颜色恢复本质上是病态逆问题，现有方法无法控制预测误差，需要从原理上理解解空间。
ai.method: 分析病态来源（光谱投影降维+未知介质参数），识别图像中自然存在的交叉像素恢复模式，并证明在理想高光谱相机下这些模式将解限制为唯一候选。
ai.result: 给出了颜色恢复适定性的充分条件，为基于第一性原理的散射环境视觉算法奠定基础。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解散射成像中颜色恢复的理论极限，指导设计更鲁棒的算法或传感器。

idea_spark.idea: 利用多光谱或高光谱相机结合空间一致性先验，实现无需标定的颜色校正。
idea_spark.risk: 理想高光谱假设在实际中难以完全满足，噪声和采样率可能破坏唯一性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从物理模型推导出可计算的约束条件，而非依赖数据驱动黑箱。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解散射成像中颜色恢复的理论极限，指导设计更鲁棒的算法或传感器。

idea_spark.risk: 理想高光谱假设在实际中难以完全满足，噪声和采样率可能破坏唯一性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从物理模型推导出可计算的约束条件，而非依赖数据驱动黑箱。

en.tldr: Presents sufficient conditions for well-posed color recovery in scattering media, proving that cross-pixel relationships uniquely determine the solution for an ideal hyperspectral camera.
en.motivation: Color recovery in scattering media is an ill-posed inverse problem; existing methods cannot control prediction error without understanding the solution space.
en.method: Identifies sources of ill-posedness (spectral projection and unknown medium parameters), discovers natural cross-pixel recovery patterns, and proves uniqueness under an ideal hyperspectral camera.
en.result: Establishes sufficient conditions for well-posed color recovery, enabling first-principles vision algorithms for scattering environments.
en.help_to_user: Clarifies theoretical limits of color recovery in scattering media, guiding design of more robust algorithms or sensors.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage multispectral/hyperspectral cameras with spatial consistency priors for calibration-free color correction.
idea_spark.risk: Ideal hyperspectral assumption is hard to meet in practice; noise and undersampling may break uniqueness.
idea_spark.inspiration: Derive computable constraints from physical models rather than relying on data-driven black boxes.

papers.title: When to Think, When to Speak: Learning Disclosure Policies for LLM Reasoning
papers.summary: In single-stream autoregressive interfaces, the same tokens both update the model state and constitute an irreversible public commitment. This coupling creates a \emph{silence tax}: additional deliberation postpones the first \emph{task-relevant} content, while naive early streaming risks premature commitments that bias subsequent generations. We introduce \textbf{\emph{Side-by-Side (SxS)}} Interleaved Reasoning, which makes \emph{disclosure timing} a controllable decision within standard autoregressive generation. SxS interleaves partial disclosures with continued private reasoning in the same context, but releases content only when it is \emph{supported} by the reasoning so far. To learn such pacing without incentivizing filler, we construct entailment-aligned interleaved trajectories by matching answer prefixes to supporting reasoning prefixes, then train with SFT to acquire the dual-action semantics and RL to recover reasoning performance under the new format. Across two Qwen3 architectures/scales (MoE \textbf{Qwen3-30B-A3B}, dense \textbf{Qwen3-4B}) and both in-domain (AIME25) and out-of-domain (GPQA-Diamond) benchmarks, SxS improves accuracy--\emph{content-latency} Pareto trade-offs under token-level proxies (e.g., inter-update waiting).

ai.tldr: 提出Side-by-Side (SxS)交错推理方法，在自回归生成中控制披露时机，通过监督微调和强化学习优化准确率与延迟的帕累托权衡。
ai.motivation: 标准自回归接口中，同一token既更新模型状态又构成不可逆公开承诺，导致“沉默税”：额外思考推迟首个任务相关内容，而早期流式输出可能产生有偏后续生成。
ai.method: SxS在相同上下文中交错部分披露与持续私有推理，仅在推理支持时才释放内容；通过构建蕴含对齐的交错轨迹，先使用SFT学习双动作语义，再用RL恢复推理性能。
ai.result: 在Qwen3-30B-A3B和Qwen3-4B上，AIME25和GPQA-Diamond基准测试中，SxS改善了准确率-内容延迟的帕累托权衡。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在LLM推理中平衡思考深度与响应速度，避免过早承诺或过度延迟。

idea_spark.idea: 将披露时机作为可学习策略，结合SFT和RL优化，可能扩展到多轮交互或长文档生成。
idea_spark.risk: 训练复杂度增加，且需要精心设计蕴含对齐轨迹以避免奖励欺骗。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类“三思而后言”启发，将内部思考与外部表达解耦，类似思维链但更灵活。

zh.tldr: 提出Side-by-Side (SxS)交错推理方法，在自回归生成中控制披露时机，通过监督微调和强化学习优化准确率与延迟的帕累托权衡。
zh.motivation: 标准自回归接口中，同一token既更新模型状态又构成不可逆公开承诺，导致“沉默税”：额外思考推迟首个任务相关内容，而早期流式输出可能产生有偏后续生成。
zh.method: SxS在相同上下文中交错部分披露与持续私有推理，仅在推理支持时才释放内容；通过构建蕴含对齐的交错轨迹，先使用SFT学习双动作语义，再用RL恢复推理性能。
zh.result: 在Qwen3-30B-A3B和Qwen3-4B上，AIME25和GPQA-Diamond基准测试中，SxS改善了准确率-内容延迟的帕累托权衡。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在LLM推理中平衡思考深度与响应速度，避免过早承诺或过度延迟。

idea_spark.idea: 将披露时机作为可学习策略，结合SFT和RL优化，可能扩展到多轮交互或长文档生成。
idea_spark.risk: 训练复杂度增加，且需要精心设计蕴含对齐轨迹以避免奖励欺骗。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类“三思而后言”启发，将内部思考与外部表达解耦，类似思维链但更灵活。

en.tldr: Proposes Side-by-Side (SxS) Interleaved Reasoning to control disclosure timing in autoregressive generation, using SFT and RL to improve accuracy-latency Pareto trade-offs.
en.motivation: In standard autoregressive interfaces, the same tokens update model state and constitute irreversible public commitments, creating a 'silence tax': extra deliberation delays first task-relevant content, while naive early streaming risks biased generations.
en.method: SxS interleaves partial disclosures with private reasoning in the same context, releasing content only when supported; constructs entailment-aligned trajectories, trains with SFT for dual-action semantics and RL to recover reasoning performance.
en.result: On Qwen3-30B-A3B and Qwen3-4B, across AIME25 and GPQA-Diamond benchmarks, SxS improves accuracy-content latency Pareto trade-offs.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to balance reasoning depth and response speed in LLMs, avoiding premature commitment or excessive delay.

idea_spark.idea: Treating disclosure timing as a learnable policy with SFT and RL could extend to multi-turn interactions or long document generation.
idea_spark.risk: Increased training complexity and need for careful entailment-aligned trajectory design to avoid reward hacking.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by 'think before you speak', decoupling internal reasoning from external expression, more flexible than chain-of-thought.

papers.title: UnAC: Adaptive Visual Prompting with Abstraction and Stepwise Checking for Complex Multimodal Reasoning
papers.summary: Although recent LMMs have become much stronger at visual perception, they remain unreliable on problems that require multi-step reasoning over visual evidence. In this paper, we present UnAC (Understanding, Abstracting, and Checking), a multimodal prompting method that strengthens reasoning for complex multimodal tasks in LMMs (e.g., GPT-4o, Gemini 1.5, and GPT-4V). To improve image understanding and capture fine details, we propose an adaptive visual prompting strategy that enables LMMs to focus on salient regions. We further design an image-abstraction prompt to effectively extract key information from images. In addition, we introduce a gradual self-checking scheme that improves reasoning by verifying each decomposed subquestion and its answer. Extensive experiments on three public benchmarks-MathVista, MM-Vet, and MMMU.

ai.tldr: 提出UnAC方法，通过自适应视觉提示、图像抽象提示和逐步自检，增强大语言多模态模型在复杂多步推理任务中的可靠性。
ai.motivation: 现有LMM在视觉感知上虽强，但在需要多步推理的复杂视觉问题上仍不可靠。
ai.method: 自适应视觉提示聚焦显著区域，图像抽象提示提取关键信息，逐步自检验证每个子问题及其答案。
ai.result: 在MathVista、MM-Vet和MMMU三个基准上验证了有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户提升LMM在复杂多模态推理任务中的准确性和可解释性。

idea_spark.idea: 结合图像抽象与分步验证，可进一步探索动态调整提示粒度或引入外部知识库。
idea_spark.risk: 逐步自检可能增加计算开销，且抽象提示可能丢失部分细节信息。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从人类逐步推理和检查答案的过程获得启发，将认知策略融入模型提示设计。

zh.tldr: 提出UnAC方法，通过自适应视觉提示、图像抽象提示和逐步自检，增强大语言多模态模型在复杂多步推理任务中的可靠性。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户提升LMM在复杂多模态推理任务中的准确性和可解释性。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从人类逐步推理和检查答案的过程获得启发，将认知策略融入模型提示设计。

en.tldr: UnAC enhances LMM reliability on complex multi-step reasoning via adaptive visual prompting, image abstraction, and stepwise self-checking.
en.motivation: Current LMMs are unreliable on problems requiring multi-step reasoning over visual evidence despite strong perception.
en.method: Adaptive visual prompting focuses on salient regions, image-abstraction prompt extracts key information, and gradual self-checking verifies each subquestion and answer.
en.result: Validated on MathVista, MM-Vet, and MMMU benchmarks.
en.help_to_user: Improves accuracy and interpretability of LMMs on complex multimodal reasoning tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Combine image abstraction with stepwise verification; explore dynamic prompt granularity or external knowledge integration.
idea_spark.risk: Stepwise checking may increase computational cost; abstraction might lose fine details.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by human step-by-step reasoning and answer verification, embedding cognitive strategies into prompting.

papers.title: Reproducing Complex Set-Compositional Information Retrieval
papers.summary: Complex information needs may involve set-compositional queries using conjunction, disjunction, and exclusion, yet it remains unclear whether current retrieval paradigms genuinely satisfy such constraints or exploit `semantic shortcuts'. We conduct a reproducibility study to benchmark major retrieval families and reasoning-targeted methods on QUEST and QUEST+Variants, and introduce LIMIT+, a controlled benchmark where relevance depends on arbitrary attribute predicates and constraint satisfaction, and less on pretrained knowledge. Our findings show that (i) on QUEST, the best neural retrievers achieve an effectiveness that is more than double what can be achieved with BM25 (Recall@100 ${>}$0.41 vs.\ 0.20), but reasoning-targeted methods like ReasonIR and Search-R1 do not outperform general-purpose retrievers uniformly; (ii) on LIMIT+, gains fail to transfer, where the strongest QUEST method collapses from Recall@100${\approx}$0.42 to below 0.02, while classic lexical retrieval gains to ${\sim}$0.96. Lastly, (iii) stratifying by compositional depth reveals a consistent degradation across all methods, where algebraic sparse and lexical methods show more stable performance while dense approaches collapse. We release code and LIMIT+ data generation scripts to support future reproducibility and controlled evaluation.

ai.tldr: 本文通过可重复性研究，发现当前检索范式在处理集合组合查询时存在语义捷径，并引入LIMIT+基准证明神经检索方法在依赖属性谓词和约束满足的任务上性能崩溃，而经典词法检索表现稳定。
ai.motivation: 复杂信息需求常涉及集合组合查询（与、或、排除），但现有检索范式是否真正满足这些约束尚不明确，可能利用了语义捷径。
ai.method: 在QUEST和QUEST+Variants上基准测试主要检索家族和推理导向方法，并引入LIMIT+基准，其中相关性取决于任意属性谓词和约束满足，减少对预训练知识的依赖。
ai.result: 在QUEST上最佳神经检索器效果是BM25的两倍以上，但推理方法未全面超越通用检索器；在LIMIT+上，QUEST最强方法召回率从0.42降至0.02以下，而词法检索升至0.96；按组合深度分层显示所有方法性能下降，稀疏和词法方法更稳定，密集方法崩溃。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解当前检索系统在集合组合查询上的真实能力，并提供一个可控基准LIMIT+用于评估和开发更鲁棒的检索方法。

idea_spark.idea: 结合词法检索的稳定性和神经检索的语义理解，设计混合模型以处理组合查询。
idea_spark.risk: 混合模型可能增加计算复杂度，且在不同组合深度上的泛化能力仍需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从词法检索在LIMIT+上的优异表现获得启发，可探索基于符号推理的检索方法。

idea_spark.risk: 混合模型可能增加计算复杂度，且在不同组合深度上的泛化能力仍需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从词法检索在LIMIT+上的优异表现获得启发，可探索基于符号推理的检索方法。

en.tldr: This reproducibility study reveals that current retrieval paradigms exploit semantic shortcuts for set-compositional queries, and introduces LIMIT+, a controlled benchmark where neural methods collapse while lexical retrieval remains robust.
en.motivation: Complex information needs often involve set-compositional queries (conjunction, disjunction, exclusion), but it is unclear whether current retrieval paradigms genuinely satisfy such constraints or exploit semantic shortcuts.
en.method: Benchmarked major retrieval families and reasoning-targeted methods on QUEST and QUEST+Variants, and introduced LIMIT+, a controlled benchmark where relevance depends on arbitrary attribute predicates and constraint satisfaction, reducing reliance on pretrained knowledge.
en.result: On QUEST, best neural retrievers achieve more than double BM25 effectiveness, but reasoning-targeted methods do not uniformly outperform general-purpose retrievers; on LIMIT+, the strongest QUEST method collapses from Recall@100≈0.42 to below 0.02, while lexical retrieval rises to ~0.96; stratification by compositional depth shows consistent degradation, with algebraic sparse and lexical methods more stable and dense approaches collapsing.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the true capability of current retrieval systems on set-compositional queries and provides a controlled benchmark LIMIT+ for evaluating and developing more robust retrieval methods.

idea_spark.idea: Combine the stability of lexical retrieval with the semantic understanding of neural retrieval to design a hybrid model for handling compositional queries.
idea_spark.risk: Hybrid models may increase computational complexity, and their generalization across different compositional depths needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the strong performance of lexical retrieval on LIMIT+, explore symbolic reasoning-based retrieval methods.

papers.title: Detecting Stealth Sycophancy in Mental-Health Dialogue with Dynamic Emotional Signature Graphs
papers.summary: As conversational AI therapists are increasingly used in psychological support settings, reliable offline evaluation of therapeutic response quality remains an open problem. This paper studies multi-domain support-dialogue evaluation without relying on large language models as final judges. We use a direct LLM judge as a baseline that reads raw dialogue text and predicts whether the target response is harmful, productive, or neutral. We find that direct LLM judges and symmetric text-similarity metrics are poorly aligned with therapeutic quality because the target label depends on clinical direction: whether the response moves the user state toward regulation or reframing, leaves it broadly unchanged, or reinforces deterioration through higher risk affect or cognitive-distortion mass. To address this issue, we propose Dynamic Emotional Signature Graphs (DESG), a model-agnostic evaluator that represents dialogue windows with decoupled clinical states and scores them using asymmetric clinical geometry. We evaluate DESG on a constructed diagnostic stress-test benchmark of 3{,}000 dialogue windows from EmpatheticDialogues, ESConv, and CRADLE-Dialogue, covering peer support, counseling dialogue, and crisis-oriented interaction. On the 600-window held-out test aggregate, DESG-Ensemble achieves 0.9353 macro-F1, exceeding ConcatANN by 1.51 percentage points, BERTScore by 19.63 points, and TRACT by 33.81 points. Feature ablations, artifact controls, a 100-window blinded adjudicator audit, and qualitative disagreement cases indicate that the clinical state manifold is the main discriminative substrate, while graph-based trajectory components provide asymmetric scoring and interpretable diagnostics rather than serving as the sole source of performance.

ai.tldr: 提出动态情感签名图（DESG）用于检测心理健康对话中的隐性谄媚，无需大型语言模型作为最终评判者。
ai.motivation: 直接LLM评判和对称文本相似度指标与治疗质量对齐差，因为目标标签取决于临床方向。
ai.method: DESG通过解耦临床状态和不对称临床几何对对话窗口进行评分。
ai.result: 在3000窗口基准上，DESG集成达到0.9353宏F1，超过ConcatANN、BERTScore和TRACT。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员和开发者评估AI心理治疗对话的质量，避免隐性谄媚。

idea_spark.idea: 利用临床状态流形和轨迹组件提供可解释的诊断。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量临床状态标注，可能难以泛化到非临床领域。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从临床心理学中的状态调节和重构概念获得灵感。

en.tldr: Proposes Dynamic Emotional Signature Graphs (DESG) to detect stealth sycophancy in mental-health dialogue without relying on LLMs as final judges.
en.motivation: Direct LLM judges and symmetric text-similarity metrics align poorly with therapeutic quality because the target label depends on clinical direction.
en.method: DESG scores dialogue windows using decoupled clinical states and asymmetric clinical geometry.
en.result: On a 3,000-window benchmark, DESG-Ensemble achieves 0.9353 macro-F1, surpassing ConcatANN, BERTScore, and TRACT.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers and developers evaluate the quality of AI therapy dialogues and detect sycophancy.

idea_spark.idea: Leverages clinical state manifold and trajectory components for interpretable diagnostics.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on high-quality clinical state annotations, which may be hard to generalize to non-clinical domains.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by concepts of state regulation and reframing in clinical psychology.

papers.title: Enhancing Agent Safety Judgment: Controlled Benchmark Rewriting and Analogical Reasoning for Deceptive Out-of-Distribution Scenarios
papers.summary: Tool-using agent systems powered by large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed across web, app, operating-system, and transactional environments. Yet existing safety benchmarks still emphasize explicit risks, potentially overstating a model's ability to judge deceptive or ambiguous trajectories. To address this gap, we introduce ROME (Red-team Orchestrated Multi-agent Evolution), a controlled benchmark-construction pipeline that rewrites known unsafe trajectories into more deceptive evaluation instances while preserving their underlying risk labels. Starting from 100 unsafe source trajectories, ROME produces 300 challenge instances spanning contextual ambiguity, implicit risks, and shortcut decision-making. Experiments show that these challenge sets substantially degrade safety-judgment performance, with hidden-risk cases remaining particularly non-trivial even for recent frontier models. We further study ARISE (Analogical Reasoning for Inference-time Safety Enhancement), a retrieval-guided inference-time enhancement that retrieves ReAct-style analogical safety trajectories from an external analogical base and injects them as structured reasoning exemplars. ARISE improves judgment quality without retraining, but is best viewed as a task-specific robustness enhancement rather than a standalone safety guarantee. Together, ROME and ARISE provide practical tools for stress-testing and improving agent safety judgment under deceptive distribution shifts.

ai.tldr: 提出ROME和ARISE两个工具，分别用于生成更具欺骗性的安全评估基准和通过类比推理增强推理时的安全判断能力。
ai.motivation: 现有安全基准主要关注显式风险，可能高估模型对欺骗性或模糊轨迹的判断能力。
ai.method: ROME通过受控重写将已知不安全轨迹转化为更隐蔽的评估实例；ARISE检索类比安全轨迹作为结构化推理示例注入。
ai.result: ROME生成的挑战集显著降低安全判断性能，隐藏风险案例对前沿模型仍具挑战；ARISE无需重新训练即可提升判断质量。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员更严格地测试和提升LLM智能体在欺骗性分布偏移下的安全判断能力。

idea_spark.idea: 结合ROME生成的对抗性示例与ARISE的推理增强，构建自适应安全训练框架。
idea_spark.risk: 过度依赖类比推理可能导致模型对未见过的欺骗模式失效，且基准重写可能引入新偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类通过类比学习应对新风险的启发，将结构化推理与对抗性测试结合。

idea_spark.idea: 结合ROME生成的对抗性示例与ARISE的推理增强，构建自适应安全训练框架。
idea_spark.risk: 过度依赖类比推理可能导致模型对未见过的欺骗模式失效，且基准重写可能引入新偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类通过类比学习应对新风险的启发，将结构化推理与对抗性测试结合。

en.tldr: Propose ROME and ARISE: ROME generates more deceptive safety benchmarks via controlled rewriting, and ARISE enhances inference-time safety judgment via analogical reasoning.
en.motivation: Existing safety benchmarks emphasize explicit risks, potentially overstating model ability to judge deceptive or ambiguous trajectories.
en.method: ROME rewrites known unsafe trajectories into more deceptive instances while preserving risk labels; ARISE retrieves analogical safety trajectories as structured reasoning exemplars.
en.result: ROME challenge sets substantially degrade safety-judgment performance, with hidden-risk cases remaining non-trivial; ARISE improves judgment quality without retraining.
en.help_to_user: Provides practical tools for stress-testing and improving agent safety judgment under deceptive distribution shifts.

idea_spark.idea: Combine ROME-generated adversarial examples with ARISE's reasoning enhancement to build an adaptive safety training framework.
idea_spark.risk: Over-reliance on analogical reasoning may fail on unseen deceptive patterns, and benchmark rewriting could introduce new biases.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by human analogical learning to handle novel risks, integrating structured reasoning with adversarial testing.

papers.title: StateVLM: A State-Aware Vision-Language Model for Robotic Affordance Reasoning
papers.summary: Vision-language models (VLMs) have shown remarkable performance in various robotic tasks, as they can perceive visual information and understand natural language instructions. However, when applied to robotics, VLMs remain subject to a fundamental limitation inherent in large language models (LLMs): they struggle with numerical reasoning, particularly in object detection and object-state localization. To explore numerical reasoning as a regression task in VLMs, we propose a novel training strategy to adapt VLMs for object detection and object-state localization. This approach leverages box decoder outputs to compute an Auxiliary Regression Loss (ARL) during fine-tuning, while preserving standard sequence prediction at inference. We leverage this training strategy to develop StateVLM (State-aware Vision-Language Model), a novel model designed to perceive and learn fine-grained object representations, including precise localization of objects and their states, as well as graspable regions. Due to the lack of a benchmark for object-state affordance reasoning, we introduce an open-source benchmark, Object State Affordance Reasoning (OSAR), which contains 1,172 scenes with 7,746 individual objects and corresponding bounding boxes. Comparative experiments on adapted benchmarks (RefCOCO, RefCOCO+, and \mbox{RefCOCOg}) demonstrate that ARL improves model performance by an average of 1.6\% compared to models without ARL. Experiments on the OSAR benchmark further support this finding, showing that StateVLM with ARL achieves an average of 5.2\% higher performance than models without ARL. In particular, ARL is also important for the complex task of affordance reasoning in OSAR, where it enhances the consistency of model outputs.

ai.tldr: 提出StateVLM，通过辅助回归损失增强视觉语言模型在机器人操作中的物体检测与状态定位能力，并发布OSAR基准。
ai.motivation: 现有视觉语言模型在机器人任务中缺乏数值推理能力，难以精确检测物体及其状态。
ai.method: 在微调阶段引入基于边界框解码器输出的辅助回归损失，推理时保持标准序列预测。
ai.result: 在RefCOCO等基准上平均提升1.6%，在OSAR基准上平均提升5.2%，并增强操作推理的一致性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过简单训练策略提升VLM在机器人感知中的数值精度。

idea_spark.idea: 将回归损失与语言模型序列预测结合，实现端到端的细粒度物体状态推理。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量边界框标注，可能限制在无标注场景的泛化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用解码器中间输出作为回归信号，避免修改推理流程，思路简洁有效。

en.tldr: Proposes StateVLM, enhancing VLM for robotic affordance reasoning via an auxiliary regression loss, and introduces the OSAR benchmark.
en.motivation: Existing VLMs lack numerical reasoning for precise object detection and state localization in robotics.
en.method: Introduces an Auxiliary Regression Loss computed from box decoder outputs during fine-tuning, while preserving standard sequence prediction at inference.
en.result: Achieves average 1.6% improvement on RefCOCO benchmarks and 5.2% on OSAR, improving affordance reasoning consistency.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how a simple training strategy can improve numerical precision in VLM-based robotic perception.

idea_spark.idea: Combining regression loss with language model sequence prediction enables end-to-end fine-grained object state reasoning.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on high-quality bounding box annotations, potentially limiting generalization to unlabeled scenes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using decoder intermediate outputs as regression signals without altering inference is a clean and effective idea.

papers.title: MiniMind-O Technical Report: An Open Small-Scale Speech-Native Omni Model
papers.summary: MiniMind-O is an open 0.1B-scale omni model built on the MiniMind language model. It accepts text, speech, and image inputs, and returns both text and streaming speech. The release includes model code, checkpoints, and the main Parquet training datasets for text-to-audio, image-to-text, and audio-to-audio training, making the complete interaction loop directly inspectable. The model uses a full MiniMind backbone as the Thinker and an independent four-layer Talker made from MiniMind blocks. Frozen SenseVoice-Small and SigLIP2 encoders provide speech and image features, which are mapped by lightweight MLP projectors and injected at modality-placeholder positions. The Talker reads a middle-layer Thinker state together with an autoregressive eight-layer Mimi-code buffer. Speaker control is handled by a dedicated speaker token, right-aligned reference codec prompts, and precomputed CAM++ speaker embeddings, so voice conditioning remains part of the audio-code context rather than a separate TTS module. With a 768-dimensional Talker, the dense and MoE variants reach average CERs of 0.0897 and 0.0900 in Thinker--Talker consistency evaluation, with overall voice-cloning similarities of 0.5995 and 0.5937. Beyond reporting a working system, the paper identifies three scale-critical design choices for small omni models: middle-layer semantic bridging, a released multimodal sequence format, and a parameter-efficient eight-codebook interface.

ai.tldr: MiniMind-O是一个基于MiniMind语言模型的0.1B规模开源全模态模型，支持文本、语音和图像输入，输出文本和流式语音。
ai.motivation: 构建一个完全开源、可检查的小规模语音原生全模态模型，并揭示小模型设计的关键选择。
ai.method: 使用MiniMind骨干作为Thinker，独立四层Talker由MiniMind块构成；冻结SenseVoice-Small和SigLIP2编码器，通过轻量MLP投影器注入模态占位符位置；Talker读取中间层Thinker状态和自回归八层Mimi码缓冲区；说话人控制通过专用说话人令牌、右对齐参考码提示和预计算CAM++说话人嵌入实现。
ai.result: 密集和MoE变体在Thinker-Talker一致性评估中平均CER分别为0.0897和0.0900，整体语音克隆相似度分别为0.5995和0.5937。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一个完全开源的小规模语音原生全模态模型，便于研究者检查完整交互流程并复现。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将Talker架构与更高效的码本压缩方法结合，以进一步降低延迟和参数量。
idea_spark.risk: 小规模模型在复杂多模态任务中可能面临性能瓶颈，尤其是在语音克隆和长序列处理上。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将说话人控制直接嵌入音频码上下文而非独立TTS模块，为简化多模态系统设计提供了新思路。

en.motivation: To build a fully open, inspectable small-scale speech-native omni model and identify critical design choices for small models.
en.method: Uses a full MiniMind backbone as Thinker and an independent four-layer Talker from MiniMind blocks; frozen SenseVoice-Small and SigLIP2 encoders with lightweight MLP projectors inject features at modality-placeholder positions; Talker reads middle-layer Thinker state and autoregressive eight-layer Mimi-code buffer; speaker control via dedicated speaker token, right-aligned reference codec prompts, and precomputed CAM++ speaker embeddings.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining the Talker architecture with more efficient codec compression methods to further reduce latency and parameters.
idea_spark.risk: Small-scale models may face performance bottlenecks in complex multimodal tasks, especially in voice cloning and long sequence processing.
idea_spark.inspiration: Embedding speaker control directly into the audio-code context rather than a separate TTS module offers a new approach to simplifying multimodal system design.

papers.title: Same Voice, Different Lab: On the Homogenization of Frontier LLM Personalities
papers.summary: LLM assistant personalities play a critical role in user experience and perceived response quality. We present a large-scale experiment of frontier LLM personalities using external ELO-based traits scoring across 144 traits. We find that all models tested converge on a form of trait expression that is systematic, methodical, and analytical and suppress traits such as remorseful and sycophantic. Moreover, models tend to diverge more in their expression of ``middle-of-distribution traits`` such as poetic or playful, but even these so-called ``creative`` models tend to have more neutral identities. These similarities suggest an implicit emergence of a standard of optimal assistant behavior. In a landscape of varied training methods, character training, therefore, stands out for its uniformity, offering insight into a tacit consensus between model developers.

ai.tldr: 前沿LLM助手的人格趋于同质化，表现为系统、有条理和分析性，抑制悔恨和谄媚等特质，即使所谓“创造性”模型也偏向中性。
ai.motivation: LLM助手的人格对用户体验和响应质量至关重要，但不同模型间的人格差异尚不明确。
ai.method: 使用基于ELO的144种特质评分，对前沿LLM进行大规模人格实验。
ai.result: 所有模型收敛于系统、有条理和分析性特质表达，抑制悔恨和谄媚；模型在“中间分布特质”上分歧较大，但整体仍偏向中性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解LLM助手人格的趋同现象，为选择或定制助手提供参考。

idea_spark.idea: 探索通过微调或提示工程刻意引入特定人格特质，以打破同质化。
idea_spark.risk: 刻意改变人格可能导致用户不适或伦理问题，需谨慎评估。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴心理学人格理论，设计更丰富、可控的LLM人格空间。

idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴心理学人格理论，设计更丰富、可控的LLM人格空间。

en.tldr: Frontier LLM assistant personalities homogenize toward systematic, methodical, and analytical traits, suppressing remorseful and sycophantic ones, with even creative models leaning neutral.
en.motivation: LLM assistant personalities critically affect user experience and perceived quality, yet cross-model personality differences are unclear.
en.method: Conducted a large-scale experiment using ELO-based scoring across 144 traits on frontier LLMs.
en.result: All models converge on systematic, methodical, and analytical trait expression, suppressing remorseful and sycophantic traits; models diverge more on mid-distribution traits but remain overall neutral.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the homogenization trend in LLM personalities, aiding model selection or customization.

idea_spark.idea: Explore deliberately introducing specific personality traits via fine-tuning or prompting to break homogenization.
idea_spark.risk: Deliberately altering personality may cause user discomfort or ethical issues, requiring careful assessment.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draw from psychological personality theories to design richer, controllable LLM personality spaces.

papers.title: ReLeaf: Benchmarking Leaf Segmentation across Domains and Species
papers.summary: Rising global food demand and growing climate pressure increase the need for sustainable, precise agricultural practices. Automated, individualized plant treatment relies on fine-grained visual analysis, yet leaf-level segmentation remains underexplored despite its value for assessing crop health, growth dynamics, yield potential and localized stress symptoms. Progress is limited by a lack of dedicated datasets, especially regarding species coverage, and by the absence of systematic evaluations of modern instance-segmentation architectures for this task. We address these gaps by surveying current data and identifying four suitable, publicly available leaf-segmentation datasets. Using them, we compare one-stage, two-stage and Transformer-based detectors and identify a YOLO26 model configuration to provide the best trade-off for real-world precision-agriculture tasks. Extensive cross-domain generalization experiments reveal substantial performance drops across plant species and recording setups, especially for models trained solely on laboratory data. To strengthen data availability, we introduce a new benchmark dataset with leaf-level masks for 23 plant species, created via semi-automatic annotation of selected CropAndWeed images. A model trained on all four existing datasets achieves a mean mAP50-95 of 83.9% across their corresponding test sets and 40.2% on our new benchmark, demonstrating improved generalization and highlighting the need for diverse leaf-segmentation datasets in robust precision agriculture.

ai.tldr: 提出ReLeaf基准，评估不同领域和物种的叶片分割方法，并发布包含23个植物物种的新数据集。
ai.motivation: 精准农业需要自动化叶片分割，但缺乏覆盖多物种的专用数据集和系统评估。
ai.method: 收集四个公开叶片分割数据集，比较单阶段、两阶段和Transformer检测器，确定YOLO26为最佳模型；通过半自动标注CropAndWeed图像创建新基准。
ai.result: 在四个数据集上训练模型达到83.9% mAP50-95，在新基准上为40.2%，跨域泛化性能显著下降。
ai.help_to_user: 提供叶片分割的基准方法和数据集，帮助用户评估和提升模型在真实农业场景中的泛化能力。

idea_spark.idea: 利用半自动标注策略快速扩展多物种叶片分割数据集。
idea_spark.risk: 新基准数据量有限，可能不足以覆盖所有实际场景。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合域适应技术或自监督学习来提升跨域分割鲁棒性。

idea_spark.inspiration: 结合域适应技术或自监督学习来提升跨域分割鲁棒性。

en.tldr: ReLeaf benchmarks leaf segmentation across domains and species, introducing a new dataset with 23 plant species.
en.motivation: Precision agriculture requires automated leaf segmentation, but lacks dedicated multi-species datasets and systematic evaluations.
en.method: Collected four public leaf segmentation datasets, compared one-stage, two-stage, and Transformer detectors, identified YOLO26 as best; created new benchmark via semi-automatic annotation of CropAndWeed images.
en.result: Model trained on four datasets achieved 83.9% mAP50-95 on their test sets and 40.2% on new benchmark; cross-domain generalization drops significantly.
en.help_to_user: Provides benchmark methods and datasets for leaf segmentation, helping users evaluate and improve model generalization in real agricultural settings.

idea_spark.idea: Use semi-automatic annotation to rapidly expand multi-species leaf segmentation datasets.
idea_spark.risk: Limited new benchmark data may not cover all real-world scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine domain adaptation or self-supervised learning to enhance cross-domain segmentation robustness.

papers.title: FACTOR: Counterfactual Training-Free Test-Time Adaptation for Open-Vocabulary Object Detection
papers.summary: Open-vocabulary object detection often fails under distribution shifts, as it can be misled by spurious correlations between non-causal visual attributes (e.g., brightness, texture) and object categories. Existing test-time adaptation (TTA) methods either depend on costly online optimization or perform global calibration, overlooking the attribute-specific nature of these failures. To address this, we propose FACTOR (counterFACtual training-free Test-time adaptation for Open-vocabulaRy object detection), a lightweight framework grounded in counterfactual reasoning. By perturbing test images along non-causal attributes and comparing region-level predictions between original and counterfactual views, FACTOR quantifies attribute sensitivity, semantic relevance, and prediction variation to selectively suppress attribute-dependent predictions-without parameter updates. Experiments on PASCAL-C, COCO-C, and FoggyCityscapes show that FACTOR consistently outperforms prior TTA methods, demonstrating that explicit counterfactual reasoning effectively improves robustness under distribution shifts.

ai.tldr: 提出FACTOR框架，通过反事实推理在测试时无需训练即可适应分布偏移，提升开放词汇目标检测的鲁棒性。
ai.motivation: 开放词汇目标检测在分布偏移下易受非因果视觉属性（如亮度、纹理）与类别间的虚假关联误导，现有测试时适应方法依赖昂贵在线优化或全局校准，忽略属性特定失败模式。
ai.method: 对测试图像沿非因果属性进行扰动，比较原始与反事实视图的区域级预测，量化属性敏感性、语义相关性和预测变化，选择性抑制属性依赖预测，无需参数更新。
ai.result: 在PASCAL-C、COCO-C和FoggyCityscapes上，FACTOR一致优于先前测试时适应方法，证明显式反事实推理有效提升分布偏移下的鲁棒性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在不重新训练模型的情况下，通过反事实推理增强开放词汇目标检测器对分布偏移的适应性。

idea_spark.idea: 利用反事实图像对（如改变亮度、纹理）进行区域级对比，无需额外训练即可识别并抑制虚假关联。
idea_spark.risk: 反事实扰动可能引入新的人工伪影，导致错误抑制；对非因果属性的定义依赖先验知识。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从因果推断中借鉴反事实推理，将其应用于测试时适应，避免在线优化开销，为鲁棒检测提供新思路。

zh.result: 在PASCAL-C、COCO-C和FoggyCityscapes上，FACTOR一致优于先前测试时适应方法，证明显式反事实推理有效提升分布偏移下的鲁棒性。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在不重新训练模型的情况下，通过反事实推理增强开放词汇目标检测器对分布偏移的适应性。

idea_spark.idea: 利用反事实图像对（如改变亮度、纹理）进行区域级对比，无需额外训练即可识别并抑制虚假关联。
idea_spark.risk: 反事实扰动可能引入新的人工伪影，导致错误抑制；对非因果属性的定义依赖先验知识。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从因果推断中借鉴反事实推理，将其应用于测试时适应，避免在线优化开销，为鲁棒检测提供新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes FACTOR, a counterfactual reasoning framework for training-free test-time adaptation to distribution shifts in open-vocabulary object detection.
en.motivation: Open-vocabulary object detection fails under distribution shifts due to spurious correlations between non-causal visual attributes (e.g., brightness, texture) and object categories; existing TTA methods are costly or overlook attribute-specific failures.
en.method: Perturbs test images along non-causal attributes, compares region-level predictions between original and counterfactual views, quantifies attribute sensitivity, semantic relevance, and prediction variation to selectively suppress attribute-dependent predictions without parameter updates.
en.result: On PASCAL-C, COCO-C, and FoggyCityscapes, FACTOR consistently outperforms prior TTA methods, demonstrating that explicit counterfactual reasoning improves robustness under distribution shifts.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to enhance open-vocabulary object detectors against distribution shifts via lightweight counterfactual reasoning without retraining.

idea_spark.idea: Use counterfactual image pairs (e.g., altering brightness, texture) for region-level comparison to identify and suppress spurious correlations without extra training.
idea_spark.risk: Counterfactual perturbations may introduce artifacts leading to incorrect suppression; defining non-causal attributes requires prior knowledge.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrows counterfactual reasoning from causal inference for test-time adaptation, avoiding online optimization overhead and offering a new perspective for robust detection.

papers.title: Preventive Maintenance of a Two-Unit Priority Standby System with Repair
papers.summary: Optimal maintenance policies play an important role in the reliability analysis of repairable systems. In this paper, we study a two-unit priority standby system with a repair facility subject to preventive maintenance. In the general case, we derive necessary and sufficient conditions under which maintenance improves the mean time to system failure. When the hazard rate of the priority unit exhibits either a bathtub-shaped or an upside-down bathtub-shaped, we establish conditions for the existence of both a threshold time beyond which maintenance enhances the mean time to system failure and an optimal maintenance time. We also derive explicit expressions for these quantities in terms of the hazard rate and the mean residual life function. Furthermore, stochastic ordering techniques are used to compare the mean lifetimes of two independent systems under this model, as well as their threshold times and optimal maintenance times.

ai.tldr: 研究了一个带有维修设施的两单元优先备用系统的最优预防性维护策略，推导了维护改善系统平均失效时间的充要条件，并针对浴盆形和倒浴盆形风险率给出了最优维护时间。
ai.motivation: 可修系统的可靠性分析中，最优维护策略至关重要，但现有研究对两单元优先备用系统的预防性维护条件尚不充分。
ai.method: 利用风险率和平均剩余寿命函数推导维护改善的充要条件，并采用随机序比较技术分析系统平均寿命。
ai.result: 得到了维护改善系统平均失效时间的阈值时间和最优维护时间的显式表达式，以及两个独立系统平均寿命的随机序比较结果。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解在何种条件下对优先单元进行预防性维护能有效延长系统平均失效时间，并给出最优维护时间的计算方法。

en.tldr: Studies optimal preventive maintenance policies for a two-unit priority standby system with repair, deriving necessary and sufficient conditions for maintenance to improve mean time to system failure, and optimal maintenance times for bathtub-shaped and upside-down bathtub-shaped hazard rates.
en.motivation: Optimal maintenance policies are crucial for reliability analysis of repairable systems, but conditions for preventive maintenance in two-unit priority standby systems are not fully explored.
en.method: Uses hazard rate and mean residual life function to derive conditions for maintenance improvement, and stochastic ordering to compare system lifetimes.
en.result: Obtains explicit expressions for threshold time and optimal maintenance time, and stochastic ordering results for two independent systems.
en.help_to_user: Helps determine when preventive maintenance on the priority unit effectively extends mean time to system failure and how to compute the optimal maintenance time.

idea_spark.idea: Combine hazard rate shape with maintenance threshold to design adaptive maintenance strategies.
idea_spark.risk: Accurate estimation of hazard rate shape in practice may be difficult, affecting threshold reliability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using stochastic ordering to compare maintenance strategies inspires broader strategy comparisons in reliability models.

papers.title: Copula-Based Endogeneity Correction for Doubly Robust Estimation of Treatment Effect
papers.summary: Doubly Robust (DR) estimation of treatment effect relies on an untestable assumption that is the absence of unobserved confounding. This assumption is par- ticularly problematic in the context of healthcare research, where variables like pre- scription refill rates serve as proxies for unobserved behaviors such as medication adherence. These proxy variables are often endogenous, exhibiting correlation with the regression error term due to unmeasured confounding or measurement error. We propose a copula-corrected doubly robust estimator that addresses endogeneity in both the treatment and outcome models without requiring instrumental variables. Gaussian copulas model the joint distribution of endogenous covariates and the error term, enabling consistent estimation while preserving the doubly robust property that requires correct specification of either the treatment or outcome model, not both. Monte Carlo simulations demonstrate that naive DR estimation exhibits substantial bias under endogeneity, whereas our corrected estimator recovers unbiased treatment effects across different data-generating processes. We apply our method to examine the effect of nutritional counseling on blood pressure using the National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey (NHANES) data. Naive DR estimation suggests counseling is associated with increased blood pressure. After copula correction, this effect becomes statistically insignificant, consistent with literature showing modest effects of nutri- Counseling in reducing blood pressure. Our methodology provides researchers with a practical tool for obtaining treatment effects in the presence of endogeneity.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于copula的双稳健估计器，用于校正由未观测混杂导致的代理变量内生性，无需工具变量。
ai.motivation: 医疗研究中代理变量（如处方续药率）常因未观测混杂或测量误差而内生，导致传统双稳健估计产生偏差。
ai.method: 使用高斯copula对内生协变量与误差项的联合分布建模，在治疗模型或结果模型之一正确设定时仍保持一致性。
ai.result: 蒙特卡洛模拟显示校正后估计无偏；NHANES数据分析表明营养咨询对血压的效应在校正后不显著。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需工具变量即可处理内生性的实用因果推断工具。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将copula校正与机器学习模型（如神经网络）结合，处理高维内生协变量。
idea_spark.risk: 高斯copula假设可能不适用于非正态或复杂依赖结构的数据。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴计量经济学中copula处理内生性的思路，拓展至双稳健估计框架。

en.tldr: Proposes a copula-corrected doubly robust estimator that addresses endogeneity in proxy variables without requiring instrumental variables.
en.motivation: Proxy variables in healthcare research (e.g., prescription refill rates) are often endogenous due to unobserved confounding or measurement error, biasing naive doubly robust estimation.
en.method: Gaussian copulas model the joint distribution of endogenous covariates and the error term, enabling consistent estimation while preserving the doubly robust property.
en.result: Monte Carlo simulations show unbiased treatment effects; NHANES analysis reveals nutritional counseling effect becomes insignificant after correction.
en.help_to_user: Provides a practical causal inference tool for handling endogeneity without instrumental variables.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining copula correction with machine learning models (e.g., neural networks) to handle high-dimensional endogenous covariates.
idea_spark.risk: Gaussian copula assumption may not hold for non-normal or complex dependency structures.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws from econometric copula methods for endogeneity, extending them to the doubly robust estimation framework.

papers.title: Generalized Function-Correcting Partition Codes
papers.summary: We introduce generalized function-correcting partition codes (GFCPCs) that simultaneously protect multiple partitions of the message space against different numbers of errors. Given partitions with respective distance requirements, a GFCPC is a systematic encoding that guarantees, for each partition, a specified minimum Hamming distance between codewords whose messages lie in different blocks. This framework unifies and generalizes both function-correcting partition codes, which protect multiple functions with a common error-correction level, and function-correcting codes with data protection, which assign different levels of protection to data and a single function. We present a multi-step construction procedure for these codes and demonstrate it with some examples. We derive general upper and lower bounds on the optimal redundancy, including the upper bound which considers the join of different combinations of the partitions. We define the distance requirement matrix $\mathcal{D}$ for the GFCPCs and use it to characterize the optimal redundancy in terms of the shortest length of an associated $\mathcal{D}$-code. For two partitions of message space over the binary field, we establish improved lower bounds on the optimal redundancy under specific neighborhood conditions on the partitions. Through several examples, we demonstrate that the proposed framework can yield strictly smaller redundancy than both the sum of the individual FCPC redundancies and the redundancy of a single FCPC designed for the join partition with the highest distance (strongest protection required).

ai.tldr: 提出广义函数校正分区码，同时保护消息空间的多个分区免受不同数量的错误影响。
ai.motivation: 现有函数校正分区码只能保护多个函数具有相同纠错级别，或为数据和单个函数分配不同保护级别，缺乏统一框架。
ai.method: 定义距离需求矩阵，通过多步构造过程生成系统编码，并推导最优冗余的上下界。
ai.result: 新框架在多个示例中比单独FCPC冗余之和或单个高保护FCPC冗余更小。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何为不同重要性的消息分区设计更高效的纠错码。

idea_spark.idea: 利用分区间的邻接条件改进下界，可能启发更紧的冗余分析。
idea_spark.risk: 构造复杂度随分区数量增加而上升，实际实现可能受限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将多目标保护统一为矩阵约束，类似多任务学习中的任务关系建模。

en.tldr: Proposes generalized function-correcting partition codes that simultaneously protect multiple partitions of the message space against different numbers of errors.
en.motivation: Existing function-correcting partition codes either protect multiple functions with a common error level or assign different levels to data and a single function, lacking a unified framework.
en.method: Defines a distance requirement matrix, constructs systematic codes via a multi-step procedure, and derives upper and lower bounds on optimal redundancy.
en.result: The new framework achieves strictly smaller redundancy than the sum of individual FCPC redundancies or a single high-protection FCPC in several examples.
en.help_to_user: Helps design more efficient error-correcting codes for message partitions with varying importance.

idea_spark.idea: Exploiting neighborhood conditions between partitions improves lower bounds, potentially inspiring tighter redundancy analysis.
idea_spark.risk: Construction complexity grows with the number of partitions, limiting practical implementation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Unifying multi-objective protection as a matrix constraint resembles task relationship modeling in multi-task learning.

papers.title: OptiLookUp: An Optical ROM-Based Loop up Table Engine for Photonic Accelerators
papers.summary: Read-only memory (ROM) provides deterministic access to predefined data mappings. Extending ROM concepts to the optical domain enables high-bandwidth, low-latency, and parallel memory access, but realizing compact and reconfigurable optical ROM remains challenging due to loss, wavelength control, and integration constraints. This work presents a high-speed, reconfigurable photonic ROM architecture implemented using integrated microring resonators (MRRs). The ROM encodes predefined input-output mappings directly in the spectral response of the photonic devices, enabling deterministic lookup-based operation without dynamic computation during readout. To improve scalability and reduce cumulative insertion loss, the architecture employs compact banked sub-arrays that are selectively addressed through an optical decoding mechanism. Reconfigurability is achieved using transistor-based optical selectors, allowing different ROM banks to be activated without physical light rerouting or interferometric structures. The proposed photonic ROM is designed and evaluated using device-level simulations based on the GlobalFoundries 45SPCLO silicon photonics platform. Simulation results demonstrate reliable operation at data rates up to 12.5 GHz, with stable light-to-current transfer characteristics obtained through integrated photodiode readout. The optical ROM can be used to implement nonlinear activation functions utilised in photonic accelerator architectures, including sigmoid, tanh, ReLU, and exponential mappings.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于微环谐振器的可重构光学只读存储器架构，用于光子加速器中的查找表操作。
ai.motivation: 光学域中实现紧凑、可重构的只读存储器面临损耗、波长控制和集成挑战。
ai.method: 采用微环谐振器编码输入输出映射，通过晶体管光学选择器实现可重构性，并利用紧凑的存储体子阵列降低损耗。
ai.result: 在GlobalFoundries 45SPCLO平台上仿真，数据速率达12.5 GHz，可实现sigmoid、tanh、ReLU等非线性激活函数。
ai.help_to_user: 为光子加速器设计提供一种低延迟、高带宽的查找表引擎方案。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将光学ROM用于光学神经网络的在线训练或权重存储。
idea_spark.risk: 实际制造中微环谐振器的工艺偏差可能导致映射精度下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受传统电子ROM的确定性访问启发，将其概念扩展到光学域以实现并行、高速查找。

en.tldr: Proposes a reconfigurable optical ROM architecture based on microring resonators for lookup table operations in photonic accelerators.
en.motivation: Realizing compact and reconfigurable optical ROM faces challenges of loss, wavelength control, and integration.
en.method: Encodes input-output mappings using microring resonators, achieves reconfigurability via transistor-based optical selectors, and reduces loss with compact banked sub-arrays.
en.result: Simulated on GlobalFoundries 45SPCLO platform, achieves data rates up to 12.5 GHz and implements nonlinear activation functions like sigmoid, tanh, ReLU.
en.help_to_user: Provides a low-latency, high-bandwidth lookup table engine design for photonic accelerators.

idea_spark.risk: Fabrication variations of microring resonators may degrade mapping accuracy in practice.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by deterministic access of traditional electronic ROM, extending the concept to the optical domain for parallel, high-speed lookup.

papers.title: AdapShot: Adaptive Many-Shot In-Context Learning with Semantic-Aware KV Cache Reuse
papers.summary: Many-Shot In-Context Learning (ICL) has emerged as a promising paradigm, leveraging extensive examples to unlock the reasoning potential of Large Language Models (LLMs). However, existing methods typically rely on a predetermined, fixed number of shots. This static approach often fails to adapt to the varying difficulty of different queries, leading to either insufficient context or interference from noise. Furthermore, the prohibitive computational and memory costs of long contexts severely limit Many-Shot's feasibility. To address the above limitations, we propose AdapShot, which dynamically optimizes shot counts and leverages KV cache reuse for efficient inference. Specifically, we design a probe-based evaluation mechanism that utilizes output entropy to determine the optimal number of shots. To bypass the redundant prefilling computation during both the probing and inference phases, we incorporate a semantics-aware KV cache reuse strategy. Within this reuse strategy, to address positional encoding incompatibilities, we introduce a decoupling and re-encoding method that enables the flexible reordering of cached key-value pairs. Extensive experiments demonstrate that AdapShot achieves an average performance gain of around 10% and a 4.64x speedup compared to state-of-the-art DBSA.

ai.tldr: 提出AdapShot，通过动态调整示例数量和语义感知的KV缓存重用，实现高效的多示例上下文学习。
ai.motivation: 现有方法使用固定示例数量，无法适应查询难度变化，且长上下文导致计算和内存开销巨大。
ai.method: 设计基于探针的输出熵评估机制动态确定最佳示例数；采用语义感知KV缓存重用策略，并引入解耦重编码方法解决位置编码不兼容问题。
ai.result: 相比最先进的DBSA，平均性能提升约10%，推理速度提升4.64倍。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户在不牺牲性能的前提下，大幅降低多示例上下文学习的计算和内存成本。

idea_spark.idea: 将输出熵作为难度指标，结合缓存重用，实现零额外开销的示例数量自适应。
idea_spark.risk: 探针评估可能引入少量额外延迟，且缓存重用策略对长序列的泛化性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类根据问题难度调整参考数量的启发，将不确定性量化与缓存技术结合。

en.tldr: AdapShot dynamically adjusts shot counts and reuses KV cache with semantic awareness for efficient many-shot in-context learning.
en.motivation: Existing methods use a fixed number of shots, failing to adapt to query difficulty, and suffer from high computational and memory costs of long contexts.
en.method: A probe-based evaluation mechanism uses output entropy to determine the optimal shot count; a semantics-aware KV cache reuse strategy with decoupling and re-encoding addresses positional encoding incompatibilities.
en.result: Achieves ~10% average performance gain and 4.64x speedup over state-of-the-art DBSA.
en.help_to_user: Helps users drastically reduce computational and memory costs of many-shot in-context learning without sacrificing performance.

idea_spark.idea: Use output entropy as a difficulty metric combined with cache reuse to achieve zero-overhead adaptive shot count.
idea_spark.risk: Probe evaluation may introduce slight latency; cache reuse generalization to very long sequences needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by humans adjusting reference quantity based on question difficulty, combining uncertainty quantification with caching techniques.

papers.title: Rational Communication Shapes Morphological Composition
papers.summary: Human languages expand vocabularies by combining existing morphemes rather than inventing arbitrary forms. Communicative efficiency shapes lexical systems at multiple levels (Gibson et al., 2019), yet morphological composition -- combining morphemes through compounding or affixation -- has rarely been modeled as a historically situated speaker choice among competing morpheme sequences, leaving unanswered why a language settles on one morpheme combination over other plausible alternatives. We ask whether a trade-off between listener recoverability and speaker production cost can predict attested compositions over contemporaneously available alternatives. Here we show, within the Rational Speech Act (RSA) framework (Frank & Goodman, 2012; Goodman & Frank, 2016) using a time-indexed lexicon constructed from Corpus of Historical American English (COHA) and Corpus of Contemporary American English (COCA), that across 4323 naturally occurring English compounds and derivations spanning 1820--2019, attested compositions are systematically ranked above unattested alternatives generated from contemporaneously available morphemes. Models integrating semantic informativeness with production cost outperform semantic-only and cost-only baselines on Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR) and top-k accuracy (Acc@k), with the advantage of the Pragmatic Speaker model ($S_1$) over the semantic-only baseline growing as the candidate set expands, where meaning alone leaves morphological choice underdetermined. These findings suggest that lexicalization reflects a communicative trade-off between expressiveness and efficiency, extending rational accounts of communication from utterance-level choice to the internal structure of words.

ai.tldr: 通过理性言语行为框架，发现英语复合词和派生词的形成反映了说话者表达成本与听者可恢复性之间的权衡。
ai.motivation: 现有研究很少将形态组合建模为历史情境下说话者在竞争性语素序列中的选择，因此无法解释语言为何选择某种语素组合而非其他合理选项。
ai.method: 基于COHA和COCA构建时间索引词典，在理性言语行为框架下比较1820-2019年间4323个英语复合词和派生词与同期可用语素生成的未证实替代方案。
ai.result: 证实组合在平均倒数排名和top-k准确率上系统性地优于未证实替代方案，且语用说话者模型在候选集扩大时优势更明显。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解语言形态组合背后的理性沟通原则，为计算语言学中的词汇化研究提供新视角。

idea_spark.idea: 利用类似方法研究中文复合词的历史演变，或设计更高效的机器翻译词汇表。
