idea_spark.risk: 过度依赖RAG可能掩盖关键信号，导致误判。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将恶意软件解释重新定义为信号提取问题，启发更简洁的生成式安全工具设计。

en.tldr: Empirical evaluation shows that RAG degrades malware explanation quality when structured VirusTotal reports are used as input.
en.motivation: To test whether RAG actually improves explanation quality in malware analysis.
en.method: Compared explanation quality with and without RAG across multiple LLMs using structured VirusTotal reports.
en.result: RAG frequently introduces distracting context and narrative noise, reducing explanation quality.
en.help_to_user: Security analysts should be cautious: RAG can be counterproductive when structured evidence is already sufficient.

idea_spark.idea: Develop an adaptive RAG gate that decides retrieval based on input structuredness.
idea_spark.risk: Over-reliance on RAG may obscure critical signals and lead to misjudgment.
idea_spark.inspiration: Reframe malware explanation as a signal-extraction problem, inspiring simpler generative security tools.

papers.title: Lifting to tensors when compiling scientific computing workloads for AI Engines
papers.summary: It has been demonstrated that specialised architectures, such as FPGAs and AMD's AI Engines (AIEs), have the potential to deliver energy and performance advantages for scientific computing. Given the integration of AIEs into AMD's CPUs, this is an interesting potential avenue especially when executing on the edge or making better use of local compute constrained resources. However, a major challenge is in enabling existing codes to run on this architecture without extensive modification. Put simply, it requires significant expertise and time to port codes to the AIE's execution model.   In this paper we explore a compilation pipeline for efficiently mapping loops in general purpose, scientific codes to AIEs. Lifting the semantics of an application into tensors, we demonstrate that this is able to capture the intention of general purpose loops annotated with OpenMP and such high-level tensor information provides a richness that is effective when mapping to the AIEs. Requiring only an OpenMP decorated loop, our approach significantly reduces code complexity when targeting the architecture. For six kernel benchmarks, representing AI and scientific computing, using our approach the NPU performs comparatively to the multicore CPU for float32, in all cases at reduced energy to solution. For two scientific computing kernels running across both the CPU and NPU together delivers up to a 40% improvement in performance and 15% reduction in energy usage compared to the CPU alone.

ai.tldr: 提出一种编译方法，通过将科学计算循环提升为张量表示，自动映射到AMD AI引擎，降低移植复杂度并提升能效。
ai.motivation: 现有科学计算代码难以移植到AI引擎架构，需要大量专家手工优化。
ai.method: 将OpenMP标注的循环语义提升为张量，利用高层张量信息指导编译映射到AI引擎。
ai.result: 在六个基准测试中，NPU在float32精度下性能与多核CPU相当，且能耗更低；CPU+NPU协同运行可提升性能40%、降低能耗15%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户了解如何通过编译技术将现有科学计算代码自动适配到AI引擎，减少手动移植工作。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将张量提升与自动微分结合，实现科学计算工作负载的全自动优化。
idea_spark.risk: 张量提升可能无法捕获复杂控制流或非规则循环结构，导致映射失败。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受OpenMP高层语义与张量表示结合启发，可进一步研究将领域特定语言（如TensorFlow）的图优化用于通用科学计算。

en.tldr: Proposes a compilation method that lifts scientific computing loops into tensor representations for automatic mapping to AMD AI Engines, reducing porting complexity and improving energy efficiency.
en.motivation: Existing scientific codes are difficult to port to AI Engine architectures, requiring extensive manual expertise.
en.method: Lifts OpenMP-annotated loop semantics into tensors, using high-level tensor information to guide compilation mapping to AI Engines.
en.result: On six benchmarks, the NPU performs comparably to multicore CPU for float32 with lower energy; CPU+NPU co-execution yields up to 40% performance improvement and 15% energy reduction.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how compilation techniques can automatically adapt existing scientific codes to AI Engines, reducing manual porting effort.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining tensor lifting with automatic differentiation for fully automated optimization of scientific workloads.
idea_spark.risk: Tensor lifting may fail to capture complex control flow or irregular loop structures, leading to mapping failures.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the combination of high-level OpenMP semantics and tensor representations, further research could apply graph optimization from domain-specific languages (e.g., TensorFlow) to general scientific computing.

papers.title: When Agents Handle Secrets: A Survey of Confidential Computing for Agentic AI
papers.summary: Agentic AI systems, specifically LLM-driven agents that plan, invoke tools, maintain persistent memory, and delegate tasks to peer agents via protocols such as MCP and A2A, introduce a threat surface that differs materially from standalone model inference. Agents accumulate sensitive context, hold credentials, and operate across pipelines no single party fully controls, enabling prompt injection, context exfiltration, credential theft, and inter-agent message poisoning. Current defenses operate entirely within the software stack and can be silently bypassed by a sufficiently privileged adversary such as a compromised cloud operator. Confidential computing (CC) offers a hardware-rooted alternative: Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) isolate agent code and data from privileged system software, while remote attestation enables verifiable trust across distributed deployments. This survey synthesizes the design space in four parts: (i) a unified taxonomy of six TEE platforms (Intel SGX, Intel TDX, AMD SEV-SNP, ARM TrustZone, ARM CCA, and NVIDIA H100 CC) covering deployment roles and performance tradeoffs; (ii) an agent-centric threat model spanning perception, planning, memory, action, and coordination layers mapped to nine security goals; (iii) a comparative survey of CC-based defenses distinguishing findings that transfer from single-call inference versus what requires new agentic designs; and (iv) six open challenges including compound attestation for multi-hop agent chains and GPU-TEE performance at LLM scale. While several hardware trust primitives appear mature enough for targeted deployments, no broadly established end-to-end framework yet binds them into a coherent security substrate for production agentic AI.

ai.tldr: 综述了机密计算在智能体AI中的应用，分析了TEE平台、威胁模型和开放挑战。
ai.motivation: 智能体AI面临提示注入、凭证窃取等新威胁，现有软件防御可被特权攻击者绕过。
ai.method: 从TEE平台分类、智能体威胁模型、防御对比和开放挑战四部分系统梳理设计空间。
ai.result: 硬件信任基元已可用于特定部署，但缺乏端到端框架。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解机密计算如何保护智能体AI的敏感上下文和凭证。

idea_spark.idea: 设计复合证明协议以验证多跳智能体链的完整性。
idea_spark.risk: GPU-TEE性能开销可能影响大规模LLM推理效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合硬件隔离与软件策略，构建生产级智能体安全基座。

en.tldr: A survey of confidential computing for agentic AI, covering TEE platforms, threat models, defenses, and open challenges.
en.motivation: Agentic AI introduces new threats like prompt injection and credential theft; software-only defenses can be bypassed by privileged adversaries.
en.method: Synthesizes the design space via a taxonomy of six TEE platforms, an agent-centric threat model, comparative defense survey, and six open challenges.
en.result: Hardware trust primitives are mature for targeted deployments but no end-to-end framework exists.
en.help_to_user: Understand how confidential computing can protect sensitive context and credentials in agentic AI.

idea_spark.idea: Design compound attestation protocols to verify integrity across multi-hop agent chains.
idea_spark.risk: GPU-TEE performance overhead may hinder large-scale LLM inference.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine hardware isolation with software policies to build a production-grade security substrate for agents.

papers.title: Zero Day Attacks: Novel Behaviour or Novel Vulnerability?
papers.summary: Zero-day attacks pose severe cybersecurity risks due to their high success rates and stealth. Because signature-based approaches struggle to detect such attacks, building Intrusion Detection Systems (IDSs) for detecting zero-day attacks is essential. We contend that for an IDS to be effective it must be grounded in an understanding of how zero-day attacks manifest in real-world networks. To this end, we review documented zero-day incidents spanning 20 years, finding that these attacks arise from the exploitation of undisclosed vulnerabilities rather than novel attack behavior. Guided by this insight, we propose a taxonomy of zero-day vulnerability types and analyze assumptions of ML-based intrusion detection approaches. Our analysis shows that incidents consistently involve vulnerability exploitation, with memory-corruption flaws being most used; additionally, attacks targeting defensive-mechanism vulnerabilities have increased in recent years. We also identify a mismatch: while incident reports emphasize vulnerability exploitation, many ML-based detectors are designed to detect hypothetical "novel behaviors" during attack execution. Our findings indicate that vulnerability-centric methods are more aligned with real-world attack mechanisms. Consequently, reliance on behavior-based detection alone may overstate zero-day detection capabilities in ML-based IDSs. We advocate for cautious interpretation of such claims and call for improved automated vulnerability detection frameworks aligned with real-world exploit characteristics. Effective defense against zero-day attacks requires prioritizing vulnerability-centeric approaches that enable early identification and mitigation across the lifecycle. The ability to detect attacks that utilize novel behaviors (Tactics, Techniques, and Procedures (TTPs)) is useful, but it does necessarily equate to the ability to detect zero-day attacks.

ai.tldr: 通过分析20年零日攻击事件，发现零日攻击源于未公开漏洞的利用而非新颖行为，现有ML检测器过度依赖行为检测，建议转向漏洞中心方法。
ai.motivation: 零日攻击成功率极高且隐蔽，签名检测失效，亟需构建有效的入侵检测系统。
ai.method: 回顾20年零日攻击事件，提出漏洞类型分类法，分析ML检测方法的假设与真实攻击机制之间的错配。
ai.result: 零日攻击始终涉及漏洞利用，内存损坏漏洞最常见；针对防御机制漏洞的攻击近年增加；行为检测与真实攻击机制不匹配。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助安全研究者理解零日攻击的本质，避免过度依赖行为检测，指导开发更有效的漏洞中心检测框架。

idea_spark.idea: 设计结合漏洞利用特征与行为异常的混合检测模型，优先识别已知漏洞类型的利用模式。
idea_spark.risk: 漏洞数据库可能不完整，新型漏洞利用模式可能被遗漏；过度依赖漏洞特征可能降低对未知漏洞的检测能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从真实攻击事件中提取漏洞利用模式，启发构建基于漏洞生命周期的早期预警系统。

en.tldr: Analyzing 20 years of zero-day incidents reveals that attacks stem from undisclosed vulnerability exploitation, not novel behavior; current ML detectors overemphasize behavior detection, urging a shift to vulnerability-centric approaches.
en.motivation: Zero-day attacks have high success rates and stealth, defeating signature-based detection, necessitating effective intrusion detection systems.
en.method: Review documented zero-day incidents over 20 years, propose a taxonomy of vulnerability types, and analyze the mismatch between ML-based detection assumptions and real attack mechanisms.
en.result: Incidents consistently involve vulnerability exploitation, with memory-corruption flaws most used; attacks targeting defensive-mechanism vulnerabilities have increased; behavior-based detection misaligns with real-world attack patterns.
en.help_to_user: Helps security researchers understand the true nature of zero-day attacks, avoid overreliance on behavior detection, and guide the development of more effective vulnerability-centric detection frameworks.

idea_spark.idea: Design a hybrid detection model combining vulnerability exploitation features with behavioral anomalies, prioritizing known vulnerability type exploitation patterns.
idea_spark.risk: Vulnerability databases may be incomplete, potentially missing novel exploitation patterns; over-reliance on vulnerability features may reduce detection capability for unknown vulnerabilities.
idea_spark.inspiration: Extracting vulnerability exploitation patterns from real incidents inspires building early warning systems based on the vulnerability lifecycle.

papers.title: Geographic Variation in Stack Overflow Code Quality: Evidence from a Cross-Regional Study of Coding Practices
papers.summary: Developers frequently reuse Stack Overflow code snippets, yet the quality of these snippets remains unevenly understood, particularly across programming languages and geographic contexts. This study investigates code quality in Stack Overflow answers from contributors located in the United States, focusing on SQL, JavaScript, Python, Ruby, and Java snippets. We evaluate four quality dimensions: reliability, readability, performance, and security. Using language-specific linting and static analysis tools, we quantify violations across states and cities, compute violation densities to enable fair regional comparison, and examine relationships between code quality and state-level diversity indicators. We further conduct inductive content analysis on code snippets from California, Utah, and North Dakota to identify qualitative patterns in code quality violations. Results show that readability violations are the most prevalent across all languages, followed by reliability, performance, and security. Common issues include improper whitespace, inconsistent formatting, program-flow errors, inefficient resource use, unsanitised inputs, and insecure dynamic evaluation. Regional analysis indicates that major technology hubs produce more parsable snippets but do not necessarily exhibit higher violation densities. States with broader access to computing devices, Internet subscriptions, higher income, and more equitable wealth distribution tend to show fewer code quality violations. Qualitative findings suggest that established technology regions often produce more complex violations, while less mature technology regions display more fundamental errors. These findings highlight the socio-technical nature of code quality in community question-answering platforms and suggest that developers should exercise caution when reusing online code snippets.

ai.tldr: 研究Stack Overflow代码片段质量的地理差异，发现可读性问题最常见，技术中心区域代码可解析性更好但违规密度不一定更低，社会经济因素与代码质量相关。
ai.motivation: 开发者频繁复用Stack Overflow代码，但代码质量在不同编程语言和地理区域中的差异尚不明确。
ai.method: 使用语言特定的静态分析工具评估美国各州和城市的SQL、JavaScript、Python、Ruby和Java代码片段在可靠性、可读性、性能和安全性四个维度的违规情况，并计算违规密度进行区域比较，同时对加州、犹他州和北达科他州的代码进行归纳内容分析。
ai.result: 可读性违规最常见，技术中心区域代码可解析性更好但违规密度不一定更低；设备访问、互联网订阅、收入和财富分配更平等的州违规更少；成熟技术区域违规更复杂，欠成熟区域错误更基础。
ai.help_to_user: 提醒开发者在复用Stack Overflow代码时注意地理和社会经济因素对代码质量的影响，谨慎选择代码片段。

idea_spark.idea: 开发一个基于地理和社会经济因素的代码质量推荐系统，帮助开发者选择更可靠的代码片段。
idea_spark.risk: 数据可能受限于美国样本，结论推广需谨慎；社会经济指标与代码质量的因果关系需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合区域多样性指标和代码质量分析，探索社区问答平台中代码质量的 socio-technical 特性。

en.tldr: Investigates geographic variation in Stack Overflow code quality, finding readability violations most common, tech hubs produce more parsable snippets but not necessarily lower violation densities, and socioeconomic factors correlate with code quality.
en.motivation: Developers frequently reuse Stack Overflow code snippets, but quality differences across programming languages and geographic contexts are poorly understood.
en.method: Used language-specific linting and static analysis tools to evaluate SQL, JavaScript, Python, Ruby, and Java snippets from US states and cities on reliability, readability, performance, and security; computed violation densities for fair regional comparison; conducted inductive content analysis on snippets from California, Utah, and North Dakota.
en.result: Readability violations most prevalent; tech hubs produce more parsable snippets but not necessarily lower violation densities; states with better device access, internet subscriptions, higher income, and more equitable wealth distribution show fewer violations; mature tech regions produce more complex violations, less mature regions show more fundamental errors.
en.help_to_user: Advises developers to consider geographic and socioeconomic factors when reusing Stack Overflow code snippets, and to exercise caution in selection.

idea_spark.idea: Develop a code quality recommendation system that incorporates geographic and socioeconomic factors to help developers select more reliable snippets.
idea_spark.risk: Data limited to US sample; generalizability requires caution; causal relationship between socioeconomic indicators and code quality needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine regional diversity indicators with code quality analysis to explore the socio-technical nature of code quality in community Q&A platforms.
